## **1. Director’s Report** 

**==> picture [245 x 235] intentionally omitted <==**

## **Prof. Amit Patra** 

Director, IIT (BHU) Varanasi 

**IT-BHU has been consistently ranked among the top engineering institutions in the country. IT-BHU became IIT (BHU), Varanasi, on June 29, 2012, by an Act of Parliament. The Institute has maintained a high academic standard and produced distinguished engineers and administrators since its inception.** 

Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi owes its existence to Mahamana Pandit Madan Mohan Malviya, Bharat Ratna, the founder of the first residential University of modern India. He foresaw the vital role of technical education in strengthening independent India and initiated engineering education at Banaras Hindu University (BHU) in 1919 with the establishment of Banaras Engineering College (BENCO). The next stage of development saw the establishment of the College of Technology (TECHNO) and the College of Mining & Metallurgy (MINMET). In 1968, the erstwhile engineering colleges of BHU, namely BENCO, MINMET, and TECHNO, were merged to form the Institute of Technology (IT-BHU). IT-BHU has been consistently ranked among the top engineering institutions in the country. IT-BHU became IIT (BHU), Varanasi, on June 29, 2012, by an Act of Parliament. The Institute has maintained a high academic standard and produced distinguished engineers and administrators since its inception. 

## **1.1. Academic Activities** 

The Institute has 15 departments and 4 interdisciplinary Schools. The current student strength is 9,084, comprising 4,903 BTech, 1,514 IDD, 89 B.Arch., 865 M.Tech., 92 M.Sc, 99 M.Pharm., and 1,532 Ph.D. students. In line with its commitment to academic excellence and institutional growth, IIT (BHU) has undertaken significant initiatives in faculty recruitment, curriculum innovation, and research facilitation. Over the last year, the Institute has successfully recruited more than 31 new faculty members across diverse departments and disciplines, thereby strengthening its academic capacity. The current strength of the Institute’s regular faculty members is 377. Additionally, three Visiting Professors, three Adjunct Faculty members, and one Associate Professor (full-time on contract) contribute to the academic activities of the Institute. 

IIT (BHU) has undertaken an extensive revision of its undergraduate curriculum in line with the recommendations of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020. The curriculum offers multi-layered flexibility in academic pathways. At the entry point, after qualifying for JEE Advanced, students are admitted according to their preference and merit to any one of the B.Tech. programs in the Institute. At the end of the fourth semester, students have the opportunity to pursue a Minor or a second major in a subject other than their parent program, as per their preference and merit. A student can also opt for an integrated dual degree (IDD) M.Tech. with a strong research component in the parent discipline at the end of 

Annual Report 2024-25 1 

Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi 

**==> picture [34 x 35] intentionally omitted <==**

the 4[th] or the 6[th] semester. The curriculum allows students to switch between Minor and Second Major disciplines, subject to the availability of seats. In all BTech program options, the 6[th] semester is a semester-long industrial, research, or Start-up internship. Following the semester-long internship, interested students can also pursue stream projects leading to a B.Tech. Honors degree. 

Multiple exit points with re-entry options is being implemented. Early exit is possible at three stages: a Certificate of Proficiency, a Diploma, or a B.Sc. in Engineering, with re-entry allowed for up to seven years. Credit transfer, online, and hybrid learning through NPTEL and SWAYAM further support flexible and multidisciplinary learning. The Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) system has been implemented at IIT (BHU) to facilitate credit transfer. To strengthen industry-relevant skills, IIT (BHU) has partnered with SARAS AI Institute to offer a foundational course in Artificial Intelligence. Another AI course has been launched for students with a programming background by the Institute, utilizing the Coforge Data & AI lab at IIT (BHU). The Institute has also introduced a course on standards and regulatory frameworks in collaboration with the Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS). 

## **1.2. Research & Development activities** 

Our Institute has a mission to fulfil the nation’s needs through Research and Innovation. Faculty members and students are engaged in advanced and innovative research under various schemes. The Institute has established Tinkering Labs in various departments to foster a culture of research among students. Students are involved in research projects from an early stage of their education. The Institute provides partial support to faculty members’ research initiatives through grants such as Seed Money and Research Support Grant. The Institute also provides Lab Grants for the upgradation of teaching labs and funds acquisitions made by the Central Instrument Facilities. 

## _**(i) Sponsored Projects and MoUs**_ 

The Institute has proven expertise in steel, advanced materials, microwave technology, electrical and electronic devices, artificial intelligence, composite materials, novel reactor design, energy sources, new drugs, and sensors/biosensors. The Institute has identified thrust areas of Research in Hydrogen Energy, Environment and Water, Healthcare, Biomedical Devices, Biosensors, Smart microgrids, Smart e-mobility, Quantum Computing, Precision Engineering, Material Science, and Drone Technologies. Various technology development and research projects are going on in these research areas. 

Significant projects have been initiated to address national needs and social issues, like developing functional materials for energy, compact hydrogen generation devices, and biofuel cells. Critical issues for studies include realtime simulation of smart grids with distributed energy resources and integration/control of renewable energy systems. Faculty members are extensively involved in designing and developing new drugs, biomimetic materials for organs, and biosensors. The Institute also works extensively in hydrogen energy, which aligns with the National Green Hydrogen Mission. The Institute is collaborating with high-tech industrial entities. These include Tata Motors, Nissan Motors, Amazon AWS Educate, Power Grid Corporation of India Ltd., the Indian Refractory Makers Association (IRMA), the Indian Pharmacopoeia Commission (IPC), NCL, CISCO, UPEIDA, ISRO, and GAIL (India) Limited, among others. 

The Institute has forged strategic international partnerships through a series of active Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) with globally recognized universities and research organizations. The Institute has signed seventeen more international MoUs in the past year. These collaborations span a wide range of academic and research domains, including cultural and religious studies, smart environmental technologies, secure communication systems, semiconductor innovation, and geodetic science. Notable partners include institutions in Germany, Denmark, the United States, Japan, Finland, Austria, Taiwan, and the United Arab Emirates. These MoUs facilitate academic exchange, joint research initiatives, student and faculty mobility, and professional development programs. These engagements significantly enhance the Institute’s global footprint and contribute to its mission of advancing interdisciplinary research and innovation aligned with national and international priorities. 

The total amount of funds the Institute receives through sponsored projects, schemes, and consultancy projects has shown a consistent upward trend over the years.IIT (BHU) Varanasi has 590 ongoing sponsored projects with a financial outlay of ₹330.47 crores. In the first quarter of  year 2025 alone, 77 new projects have been sanctioned so far. The number of new projects sanctioned in the years 2021 to 2024 were 62, 115, 94, and 134, respectively. Additionally, the total financial outlay of new projects for 2025 stands at ₹79.03 crores (Figure 1a and 1b). 

The number of publications from IIT (BHU) has increased almost yearly since its conversion from IT-BHU in 2012 (Figure 1c). Moreover, citations have more than doubled (Figure 1d) in the last five years (on average, a 22% increase yearly). Hence, we expect a significant increase in publications and citations in the year 2025 as well.

## _**(vi) Intellectual Property Rights, Testing, and Consultancy**_ 

IIT (BHU) Varanasi has witnessed a consistent growth in consultancy activities from 2018 to 2025. The number of consultancies increased from 894 in 2018 to 1,745 in 2024, with 108 consultancies recorded (Figure 2). Correspondingly, the financial outlay rose from ₹14.04 crores in 2018 to ₹36.46 crores in 2024, with ₹7.78 crores already achieved in 

2025. The data for 2025 (marked with *) reflects figures up to March 2025, and is expected to rise further by year-end. This growth underscores the Institute’s commitment to applied research, industry collaboration, and societal impact. 

The IPR Cell has emerged as a pivotal force in driving innovation over the past year, with 169 patents filed, 172 published, and 85 awarded between the last convocation and now, showcasing a vibrant research culture. This progress is vividly illustrated in Figure 3, highlighting the upward trend in patents granted over the last five years: 2 in 2021, 40 in 2022, 44 in 2023, 92 in 2024, and 64 in 2025. The green bar and upward arrow underscore this remarkable growth, reflecting a robust and expanding intellectual property ecosystem.

## **1.2. Research Infrastructure, Centers, and Hubs** 

## _**Central Instrumentation Facility (CIF)**_ 

CIF provides advanced instruments and expert support for research and industrial R&D, featuring tools like MPMS, SEM, TEM, NMR, XRD, AFM, and prototyping systems. 

## _**Center for Computing and Information Services (CCIS)**_ 

CCIS offers high-end servers, licensed software, and network services to support academic and research activities, with growing capabilities to meet evolving institutional needs. 

## _**Precision Engineering Hub (PEH)**_ 

PEH established in March 2021, supports product development aligned with Make in India and Atmanirbhar Bharat, equipped with advanced technologies like CNC machines, 3D printers, and Raman spectroscopy. 

## _**Design and Innovation Center (DIC)**_ 

DIC promotes interdisciplinary innovation across technology, humanities, and the arts, with spokes at IIIT Allahabad, MNNIT Allahabad, and the University of Allahabad, and houses labs like the Graphics and Digital Media Lab and the Design Café. 

## _**Artificial Intelligence and Data Science Initiatives**_ 

IIT (BHU), Varanasi, is steadily strengthening its position as a hub for Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Data Science research and education. Recognizing the transformative potential of AI in reshaping industries, governance, and society, the Institute has launched dedicated initiatives that integrate advanced research, cutting-edge infrastructure, skill development, and industry collaboration. These initiatives aim not only to advance scientific knowledge but also to equip students with skills and capabilities that are future-ready. 

## _**Coforge Data & AI Lab**_ 

A landmark step has been the establishment of the Coforge Data & AI Lab through a formal Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Coforge under its CSR initiative. The Lab has been envisioned as a Center for excellence in AI and Data Science education and applied research. It provides students with 

- i. Real-world exposure to industry problems and practices, 

- ii. Skill development programs aligned with emerging AI technologies, 

4 

Annual Report 2024-25 

Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi 

**==> picture [34 x 35] intentionally omitted <==**

- iii. Collaborative research opportunities with industry experts and faculty, and 

- iv. A platform for fostering responsible and ethical AI practices. 

The Lab’s vision is to build a future-ready, inclusive, and innovation-driven AI ecosystem at IIT (BHU). Its mission spans four pillars: innovating pioneering AI technologies, educating future leaders, fostering interdisciplinary collaborations, and promoting ethical and transparent use of AI. Through this initiative, IIT (BHU) students are being prepared to address societal challenges and industry demands in healthcare, energy, mobility, financial systems, and governance. 

## _**Supercomputing Center**_ 

IIT (BHU)’s PARAM Shivay supercomputing center, inaugurated in 2019 under the National Supercomputing Mission and Make in India initiative, offers 833 TeraFLOPS of processing power, 60% for local research and the rest for national academic use, powered by a sophisticated CPU-GPU architecture and open-source software. 

## _**Malaviya Center of Excellence for Defense Corridor**_ 

The Malaviya Center of Excellence for Defense Corridor at IIT (BHU), established through a ₹69 crore grant from the Uttar Pradesh Government and in partnership with UPEIDA, serves as a key R&D and skill development hub for defense industrial needs. 

## _**ISRO Regional Academic Center for Space**_ 

The ISRO Regional Academic Center for Space (RAC-S) promotes space technology research across UP, MP, and Chhattisgarh through joint projects, student research, and academic events, supporting both short-term and long-term initiatives. 

## _**Sustainable Coal Mining in Northern Coalfields Limited**_ 

IIT (BHU), Varanasi and NCL joined hands in 2019 to ensure a robust, mutually beneficial Industry-Institute partnership in the interest of mineral conservation, mine productivity, and advancement of clean technologies in the energy sector. Through this collaboration, NCL also ensures social upliftment of the region surrounding Varanasi, Singrauli, and Sonbhadra by carrying out dedicated CSR / welfare projects. 

## _**Collaborative research center of the Indian Pharmacopoeia Commission**_ 

The center focuses on developing cost-effective methods for pharmaceutical analysis and APIs, contributing to Atmanirbhar Bharat and supporting standards in pharmacovigilance and medical devices. 

## _**Center of Excellence in Refractories**_ 

The Center of Excellence in Refractories at IIT (BHU) is a self-sustaining unit for hands-on ceramic research and industrial training, driving innovation in emerging technologies and supporting national missions like Make in India and Atmanirbhar Bharat. 

## _**Center of Energy and Resource Development (CERD)**_ 

CERD established under MHRD’s FAST scheme, conducts cutting-edge research in sustainable energy systems, including solar technologies, fuel cells, and hydrogen energy, serving rural, urban, and industrial sectors. 

## **Center of Excellence on Machine Tools Design (CoEMTD)** 

The Center of Excellence on Machine Tools Design (CoEMTD) at IIT (BHU), established in collaboration with HMT and the Ministry of Heavy Industries, aims to strengthen India’s capital goods sector through Make in India initiatives, offering advanced facilities for machine tool design, testing, skill development, and support for tech-based startups. 

## _**DRDO Industry Academia-Center of Excellence (DIA-CoE) IIT (BHU) Varanasi**_ 

The DRDO Industry-Academia Center of Excellence (DIA-CoE) at IIT(BHU) focuses on advanced research in Powder Metallurgy, Functional Materials, and High-Power Microwave Devices, fostering collaborative innovation for indigenous defense technologies under the Atmanirbhar Bharat initiative. 

## _**I-Data Analytics and Predictive Technologies (I-DAPT)**_ 

The I-DAPT Hub Foundation at IIT (BHU), established under DST’s National Mission on Interdisciplinary CyberPhysical System (NM-ICPS) mission, focuses on Data Analytics and Predictive Technologies across five thrust areas, Telecommunications, Power, Defense R&D, Road Transport, and Health, supporting Digital India through smart infrastructure and societal outreach.

Annual Report 2024-25 

Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi 

**==> picture [34 x 35] intentionally omitted <==**

## _**Virtual Sustainability Development Center (SDC**_ 

The SDC at IIT (BHU) has been established to harness the Institute’s strengths in green, clean, and sustainable technologies, creating a healthy environment and promoting sustainable industrial practices, carbon capture, and related initiatives. Guided by its vision, the Center aims to enhance the quality of life (social dimension), conserve the environment by minimizing human and industrial impacts, pursue energy independence through the efficient utilization of green and clean energy sources, and contribute to the national economy by advancing technology-driven solutions. In collaboration with the Teaching Learning Cell (TLC) of the Institute, SDC regularly organizes training programs, workshops, and awareness activities aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs 2030) for students, faculty, and external stakeholders. The Center also utilizes the open-source Sustainability Impact Assessment (SIA) tool (developed by Wexsus & SDSN) to evaluate sustainability literacy, identify knowledge gaps, design action strategies, and disseminate knowledge through targeted programs and community engagement. The Sustainable Development Team actively works across key focus areas, including Green Mobility, Water Sustainability, Renewable Energy, Smart Cities, and Sustainable Industry, thereby positioning the Center as a catalyst for advancing sustainabilitydriven education, research, and societal impact. 

## _**National Facility for Battery Research**_ 

The Institute has been entrusted with the mandate to establish a National Facility for Battery Research, under the prestigious support of the Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF) under the Government of India’s flagship Mission for Advancement in High-impact Areas (MAHA) focused on electric mobility. IIT (BHU) is one of the three premier institutions chosen to lead this mission. It will position IIT (BHU) as a global leader in battery innovation. The facility aligns with Bharat’s National Mission on Transformative Mobility and Energy Storage in line with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). 

## _**Ideation, Innovation and Incubation Foundation (I-3F)**_ 

Ideation, Innovation and Incubation Foundation (I-3F), earlier known as Technology Innovation and Incubation Center (TIIC), is an umbrella organization at IIT (BHU), Varanasi for fostering an entrepreneurial ecosystem and nurturing startups in the East UP region. It administers various units that provide ‘Start to Scale’ support for entrepreneurship and facilitates research activities to convert into commercial ventures. Different units under I-3F are: 

_NCL-IIT(BHU) Incubation Center:_ NCL-IIT (BHU) Incubation Center (NIIC) is a collaboration of the Indian Institute of Technology (Banaras Hindu University) and Northern Coalfields Limited. The Center is a Technology Business Incubator for fostering entrepreneurship and nurturing tech start-ups of IIT (BHU), Varanasi. The business incubator provides ‘Start to scale’ support for technology-based entrepreneurship and facilitates the conversion of research activity. 

_RKVY-RAFTAAR Agri-Business Incubator (R-ABI):_ R-ABI is a scheme funded by the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare (MoA& FW) working closely with other incubators. This scheme aims to strengthen the infrastructure in agriculture and allied areas to promote agripreneurship and agri-business by providing financial support and nurturing the incubation ecosystem in and around Uttar Pradesh. 

_CISCO Thing Qbator Makerspace Program:_ As a part of a CSR initiative, Cisco Systems, along with the NASSCOM Foundation, has established a “thingQbator” makerspace at IIT (BHU). This AI and IoT-based Makerspace program helps accelerate innovation and entrepreneurship among the IIT (BHU) Varanasi student community. Students not only play with the ideas but become creative problem solvers and strengthen the start-up ecosystem of India. 

_E-Cell:_ The Entrepreneurship Cell (E-Cell) at IIT(BHU), run by students, fosters a vibrant startup ecosystem through competitions, workshops, and its flagship Founder’s Speak series, while its Research Innovation and Entrepreneurship Unit (RIEU) promotes industry-linked, innovation-driven research. 

The IIT (BHU) Foundation will fund 8 faculty-led startups with a total support of USD 30,000, reflecting strong alumni and institutional backing for innovation. Furthermore, the Institute is poised to incubate 8–10 additional startups, led by faculty and students, through the Joint Incubation Center established in collaboration with Union Bank of India. 

## **1.3. Outreach & Social Responsibility** 

## _**Unnat Bharat Abhiyan**_ 

Unnat Bharat Abhiyan (UBA) is a Ministry of Education initiative aimed at addressing the technological challenges faced by ordinary people. This project is a joint venture of all IITs and many other Institutes of National Importance. UBA is conceptualized as a movement to connect institutes of higher education with local communities to address the development challenges of rural India through appropriate technological inventions. IIT (BHU), Varanasi, is also 

6 

Annual Report 2024-25 

Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi 

**==> picture [34 x 35] intentionally omitted <==**

identified as one of the Regional Coordinating Institutions (RCIs) due to its experience and infrastructural competence. Several villages in and around Varanasi and Mirzapur have been adopted, and IIT(BHU) students are actively involved in counseling and providing the necessary technical inputs (such as preservation and promotion of rural crafts, advice for sanitation and hygiene, rainwater harvesting, solar lights for the community, forestation, drinking water quality, etc.) to the villagers. 

## _**Outreach and Industry Relations Portfolio**_ 

For the first time in its history, the Institute has introduced a dedicated Outreach and Industry Relations portfolio, which was formally initiated in January 2025 led by a Professor In-charge, this initiative is envisioned through three major lenses: Professional Development, Social Development and Industrial Development. Under the Outreach for Professional Development, special emphasis has been placed on student training and placements as well as training of professional engineers. 

## **1.4. Shreenivas Deshpande Library & E-Resources** 

The library system at the Indian Institute of Technology (BHU), Varanasi, comprises the Shreenivas Deshpande Library in addition to five departmental libraries, which collectively facilitate the educational, research, and outreach initiatives of the institution. In addition to possessing an extensive physical collection exceeding 150,000 volumes of books, journals, theses, reports, and pamphlets, the library system also grants access to over 15,000 electronic journals and more than 7,000 electronic books, e-standards, and databases pertinent to the fields of science, engineering, and technology. The library offers a variety of facilities, including reading rooms, access to a Digital Library, a web-based Online Public Access Catalogue (OPAC), remote access to electronic resources, a discussion room, and reference services that are integral to research and teaching techniques. Furthermore, the library has established the Indian Research Information Network System (IRINS), which serves as a database of the profiles of the Institute’s researchers and includes an Institutional Repository. The library further enhances research activities by offering a suite of Research Support Tools, which encompasses Anti-Plagiarism software, Grammarly, EndNote, CSD-Materials, Overleaf, ChemDraw, and various reference management tools. The library has recently implemented RFID for library transactions. 

## **1.5. Alumni Connect, Endowment, Scholarship, and Resource Generation** 

The Institute is proud of its Alumni Community, which provides invaluable support and guidance. The office of the Dean (Resource and Alumni) has organized frequent engaging sessions, both online and in person, to fortify the bonds between alumni and students. A key milestone this year: Our Alumni Registration Portal now boasts 19,499+ registered members, enriching our database for seamless global communication. 

To keep our alumni informed about the Institute’s vibrant pulse, the Student Alumni Interaction Cell (SAIC), under the guidance of the Dean of Resource and Alumni, regularly publishes its monthly newsletter, Alma Communiqué. This newsletter has reached over 4,000 readers worldwide, featuring stories from students, alumni, and the Institute. SAIC’s website (saic.iitbhu.ac.in) served as the hub for services and updates, hosting over 14,000 users and generating more than 51,000 page views. Through targeted social media campaigns, we added over 6,300 members to the portal, bringing the total number of registrations to 19,500+.   Moving forward, with UI/UX and front-end complete on a revamped SAIC site, backend development will soon knit our network even tighter. 

The annual Student Alumni Mentorship Program, aimed at providing personalized guidance and insights to students from the alumni, witnessed registrations from 450+ students who received one-to-one guidance from 80+ alumni mentors across 12 diverse domains. The Ask the Alumni initiative, specially curated for first-year students, targeting their holistic development, drew over 320 student registrants and more than 70 alumni experts. Connect and Learn, the Student Alumni Meetup, experienced surging participation, with over 250 students and more than 70 alumni connecting across eight cities. Alumni-guided mock interviews were conducted for the intern and placement seasons, equipping over 700 students via 250+ alumni across eight domains, with a total of 830+ interviews in both drives. 

The Institute witnessed 11 mesmerizing reunions, bringing families of 550+ alumni together to reminisce about fond memories while establishing fresh connections with the Institute. The Alumni Sports Meet, in conjunction with Spardha and the Sports Council, bridged generations through spirited interactions between students and alumni. 

Alumni generosity propelled us forward: From April 1, 2024,  we received about 29 major endowment scholarships, medals, awards, and other donations, totaling over Rs. 32 crores. Highlights span scholarships like the Aditya Gupta Fund (Rs. 30 lakhs) for Metallurgical Engineering students, STUTI Bajaj Memorial (Rs. 30 lakhs), IIT (BHU) Foundation contributions exceeding Rs. 42 lakhs etc. Visionary pledges shone brightest: Raj Yavatkar’s Rs. 13 crores for the Yavatkar Convention Center; Dave Goswami’s Rs. 13 crores for the Dev and Vardhana Goswami Lecture Hall Complex;

Dr. Hiralal and Patricia Fotedar’s Rs. 2 crores for an Endowed Chair in Metallurgy, Materials Science, and Engineering; and Prabhakar and Srilatha Raghavan’s Rs. 1 crores for the M. Ramakrishnan and Malini Entrepreneurship Fund. In addition to the above, IIT (BHU) signed the following landmark MoUs in 2024 to 2025: with Raj Yavatkar (February 19, 2025) for the Yavatkar Convention Center; and Tata Steel Foundation for the Scholarship Award Program, alongside HSIL Corporate Social Responsibility Foundation for the Dr. R.K. Somany Memorial Scholarships. 

A delegation from IIT (BHU) Varanasi, led by Director, concluded a productive U.S. visit, strengthening alumni ties and academic collaborations. Alumni meets were held in New Jersey, New York, Memphis, and Michigan, alongside participation in the Sci-ROI Annual Meeting 2024 and the PAN IIT World of Technology (PIWOT-2025) conference. Academic visits to the University of Michigan, Purdue University, and the University of Chicago facilitated potential partnerships in research and student exchange. Several MoUs were signed with donors for faculty chairs, startup grants, scholarships, and AI and data science research. Alumni donations—both in number and value—have seen a notable rise. In a historic first, the Alumni Association of IIT (BHU) (AASSII) conducted elections to form its inaugural Board of Directors, marking a new chapter in alumni engagement. 

## **1.6. Infrastructure Development** 

The following major construction projects are currently underway or have recently been completed at the Institute. 

- a) Construction of the Academic Building for the Department of Architecture, Planning & Design and Naresh C. Jain School of Decision Science & Engineering (G+4) is complete. 

- b) Construction of Boys Hostel-I (S+7) and Dining Block (G+4 floor) is underway. 

- c) Construction of Girls Hostel-II (S+7) 

- d) Construction of Malviya Student Activity and Computing Center (MSACC). 

- e) Construction of Centenary Defense and Precision Engineering Hub (CDPEH) (G+9) is in the pipeline. 

- f) Construction of Centenary Innovation and Research Park (CIRP) (G+9) is in the pipeline. 

- g) Construction of Type-II (80 nos. Apartments) and Type-III (40 nos. Apartments) Staff Housing (Stilt+10). 

- h) Vertical extension for the school of Biochemical building and the school of Biomedical engineering. 

- i) Vinod Ghai Centenary Center (G+1), having foundation design for (G+3) storey of E- E-waste management and recycling in the Department of Metallurgical Engineering. 

- j) Road Widening and beautification of bituminous roads (from Hyderabad Gate to Department of Computer Science & Engineering Crossing and from IIT Crossing to the School of Materials Science and Technology (SMST) Crossing). 

## **1.7. Training and Placement** 

The Training and Placement Cell (TPC) of IIT (BHU) Varanasi plays a crucial role in facilitating placements for graduating students and preparing them for industry. During the 2024– 25 academic session, approximately 398 companies visited the campus, conducting interviews and extending 1,405 offers, including four international offers. These companies represented a wide spectrum of sectors, including Core Engineering, IT & IT-enabled Services, Manufacturing, Consultancy, Finance, Management, and R&D. The average CTC stood at ₹24.56 LPA, with the highest package reaching ₹2.20 Crore. Notably, the number of visiting companies rose by ~13.06% compared to 2023–24. 

In addition to final placements, the Cell actively facilitated internships and training programs, collaborating with reputed organizations to provide students with practical exposure. In the 2024–25 session, the Cell successfully arranged 502 paid internships, including 01 international internship offer. 

Figure 4. Placement data from 2022 to 2025. 

8 

Annual Report 2024-25 

Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi 

**==> picture [34 x 35] intentionally omitted <==**

## **1.8. Students’ Activities** 

The Institute nurtures the holistic development of students through various initiatives, including the IIT (BHU) Gymkhana, different councils, the students’ parliament, and other student groups. IIT (BHU) is a residential institute, and hostel facilities include well-managed mess services in every hostel, including one South Indian and two Satwik messes for pure vegetarians. The campus facilities include the Ramesh Srinivasan Student Activity Center for indoor games, a top-notch gym, and three playgrounds: Techno Pavilion, Gymkhana, and Rajputana, offering excellent outdoor game amenities. Students Gymkhana have five vibrant councils: Cultural, Science and Technology, Social Service, Film and Media, and Games and Sports. 

## _**Cultural Council**_ 

The Cultural Council of IIT (BHU), Varanasi, celebrated a year of creativity, excellence, and vibrant cultural achievements. Aagman 24, the intra-freshers’ competition, saw enthusiastic participation from over 821 students. The Council organized celebrations such as Janmashtami, Janjatiya Gaurav Divas, Constitution Day, and Bharatiya Bhasha Utsav, alongside events including a Charcoal Art Workshop by Nirdosh Singh and Le Début by the Literary Club. Council clubs secured 25+ awards at outstation competitions. The year also marked the launch of the first General Championship Culturals, with 850+ participants in 25+ events, and the 13[th] IITBHU MUN, which hosted 200+ delegates nationwide. Kashiyatra 25, the Annual Socio-Cultural Fest, themed Sanskriti Pravah, received endorsements from renowned artists. IIT(BHU) secured 8th place at the Inter IIT Cultural Meet 7.0 hosted by IIT Patna, achieving 7 positions across various events among 23 participating IITs. 

## _**Science and Technology Council**_ 

The Science and Technology Council at IIT (BHU), Varanasi, continued its tradition of excellence with several notable achievements. At the Inter IIT Tech Meet 13.0, the Institute won three medals, including two gold and one bronze. The Robotics Club also advanced to the national finals of DD Robocon. The Council pushed forward innovations, such as UAV systems, that qualified for ISRO’s IRUCO 2025. It successfully hosted Technex 2025, the 86th edition of Asia’s oldest techno-management festival, attracting widespread participation nationwide. Additionally, the CyberSec vertical of COPS organized Kashi CTF, the Institute’s first international Capture the Flag event. This event drew over 3,000 participants worldwide and led to IIT (BHU) achieving its first-ever 1[st] place finish in an internationally rated cybersecurity competition, SpookyCTF 2024. 

## _**Social Service Council**_ 

The Social Service Council promotes interactions between students and residents from nearby impoverished localities. The Council also organized the Kashi Utkarsh Club’s Medical Camp and Sangharsh, an inter-basti sports event; the Sahyog Club’s Gram Mela; as well as the Social Project Club’s Abhipraya and industrial visits. The Institute’s flagship socio awareness festival, Jagriti 2025, was organized on the theme Architects of Sapience. 

## _**Film and Media Council**_ 

The Film and Media Council brought digital storytelling to life with hands-on workshops, photo walks, media and video walks, design marathons, and animation events. The Council bagged the Bronze Cup for overall Digital Arts at Inter IIT Cultural Meet 7.0, with a Bronze in Animation, a Gold in Online Photo Story, a Bronze in Street Photography, and a 4th place in the Photoshop Battle. Every year, the Council hosts India’s largest Digital Arts Festival, featuring workshops by creators such as Doodlenath, Shubham Sharma, and Joey. 

## _**Games and Sports Council**_ 

The Games and Sports Council conducted a series of events, including the Intra Freshers’ tournament, National Sports Day, General Championship, Inter-hostel Annual Athletics Meet, Swachhata Run, and Summer Camp for various sports within the Council. The flagship events, Saturnalia and Spardha 2024, were organized successfully, with a footfall of 5000 and 2500, respectively, all over the country. Our sports contingent has brought us immense pride this year with a truly remarkable performance at the Inter IIT Sports Meet, jointly organized by IIT Kanpur and IIT Indore. From our earlier standing of 12[th] , we have risen to secure the 7[th] overall position among all 23 participating IITs. Among the many shining moments, we proudly celebrate the Gold and Silver in Volleyball (Men’s and Women’s, respectively); a Gold in Basketball; a Silver in Cricket; and one silver, one bronze, and a Fourth position in Weightlifting. Our Aquatics team made waves with two silver and two bronze medals, along with an inspiring second place in March Past. 

9 

Annual Report 2024-25 

Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi 

**==> picture [34 x 35] intentionally omitted <==**

## **1.9. Concluding Remarks and Future Plans:** 

IIT (BHU) Varanasi is building on its strengths and transforming itself to meet future challenges. The highlights of the achievements of IIT (BHU) Varanasi are: 

**Infrastructure Enhancements:** Sanctioned a ₹464.20 crore HEFA loan to commence seven major projects, including the Centenary Innovation and Research Park (CIRP), Centenary Defence and Precision Engineering Hub (CDPEH), Malviya Student Activity and Computing Center (MSACC), two new boys’ hostels, an academic block, and staff residential apartments; initiated student life improvements in 17 hostels; begun Gymkhana renovations, including a world-class G-11 auditorium, state-of-the-art synthetic athletics court at Techno Pavilion, phased air-conditioning in classrooms/ common rooms, and preparation for inaugurating a fully air-conditioned classroom complex. 

**Academic Reforms and Programs:** Introduced a comprehensive undergraduate curriculum revision (effective from 2025 session), including four academic tracks after the 4th semester, mandatory 6th-semester internships, and multiple exit options (Interim Leaving Certificate after 1 year, Diploma after 2 years, B.Sc. in Engineering after 3 years); launched a new M.Tech program bridging engineering and medical sciences; started a part-time executive PhD program; partnered with SARAS AI Institute for a foundational AI course and with BIS for standards/regulatory frameworks; established the Student Travel Grant Scheme (up to ₹2,00,000 for Ph.D. students and ₹60,000 for Master’s to attend conferences); and initiated classroom digitization to modernize teaching-learning. 

**Research and Development:** The Institute has shown remarkable growth in R&D activities, evident from 590 ongoing sponsored projects with a financial outlay of Rs. 330.47 crores, 77 new projects sanctioned in 2025 (till September), consistent increases in publications and citations, and consultancies worth Rs. 36.46 crores in 2024 (Rs. 7.78 crores in 2025 till September). Initiatives to boost R&D include Institute Post-doctoral fellowships, Challenge grants for interdisciplinary projects, Lab Grants for teaching lab upgrades, and Seed Money/Research Support Grants for faculty; the IPR Cell filed 169 patents, published 172, and awarded 85 since the last convocation, with a five-year trend showing 2 in 2021, 40 in 2022, 44 in 2023, 92 in 2024-25. 

**Ranking:** IIT (BHU) Varanasi ranked 10th in NIRF Engineering (2025) and debuted at 22nd in NIRF Research Institutions, reflecting strengthened teaching and research that will boost recruitment and industry partnerships. IIT (BHU) Varanasi has continued to demonstrate its academic and research prowess in the QS World University Rankings 2026, securing a global rank of 566 and a national rank of 14th among Indian institutions, with the 47th rank globally in Citations per Faculty (improved from 48th), 401–550 in QS Subject Rankings 2025, and 61st in Asian University Rankings for Southern Asia. 

**Training and Placement:** In the academic session 2024–25, 398 companies visited the campus, extending 1,405 offers (including four international offers), with an average CTC of Rs. 24.56 LPA and the highest package of Rs. 2.20 crore, representing a 7.66 percent increase from 1,305 offers in 2023–24. 

**Entrepreneurship and Incubation:** Created the position of Professor In-Charge for Entrepreneurship and Incubation; earmarked 1% of the annual budget for incubation activities; inspired faculty/student ventures, with IIT (BHU) Foundation funding 8 faculty-led startups (USD 30,000 total); established a Joint Incubation Center with Union Bank of India to support 8–10 additional startups. 

**National Facilities and Centers:** Entrusted with establishing the National Facility for Battery Research under ANRF’s MAHA mission for electric mobility, aligning with national sustainability goals; set to launch the Center of Excellence for Tunnelling & Underground Space Engineering with DMRC. 

**Outreach and Partnerships:** The Institute has signed 17 new international MoUs with partners in Germany, Denmark, the US, Japan, Finland, Austria, Taiwan, and the UAE, plus domestic collaborations like the U.S. delegation visit yielding agreements for faculty chairs, startup grants, scholarships, and AI research, facilitating enhanced state-academiaindustry ties. 

**Student Activities:** Student activities fostered holistic growth via Gymkhana councils, with cultural highlights like Kashiyatra’25 (8th in Inter-IIT Cultural Meet), tech feats like Technex 2025 and 1st in SpookyCTF 2024, social initiatives like Jagriti 2025, film/media’s Bronze Cup, and sports’ 7th in Inter-IIT (golds in Volleyball/Basketball). 

**Alumni Engagement:** Alumni engagement grew with 19,500+ portal registrations, 11 reunions (550+ families), Rs. 32 crore donations (e.g., Rs. 13 crore each for Yavatkar Center and Goswami Complex), and AASSII’s first Board elections; SAIC facilitated 450+ mentorships and 830+ mock interviews. 

10 

Annual Report 2024-25 

Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi 

**==> picture [34 x 35] intentionally omitted <==**

## _**Future plans**_ 

**Infrastructure and Campus:** Complete ongoing HEFA projects to transform research capacity and student facilities; expand air-conditioning in hostels and classrooms; scale Gymkhana renovations for enhanced cultural and sports amenities; develop a Green Campus Initiative with solar-powered facilities, rainwater harvesting, and zero-waste systems to support sustainability goals. 

**Academic and Student Support:** Fully implement the 2025 UG curriculum revisions to boost employability through experiential learning and multidisciplinary options; extend the part-time executive PhD and new M.Tech programs; broaden AI and standards courses for industry relevance; increase utilization of travel grants to foster global research exposure; introduce postgraduate specializations in high-demand fields like sustainable energy, quantum computing, and data science; partner with international MoU institutions for joint dual degrees and exchanges, targeting 20% increase in outbound mobility. 

**Faculty Recruitment and Development:** Increase faculty numbers to 650 from the current 377 within three years; expedite ongoing interviews and advertisements, targeting completion within three months per cycle; introduce annual training for faculty/staff in NEP-aligned pedagogy and industry trends, including international workshops via MoUs. 

**Research and Innovation:** Capitalize on significant opportunities in chip design, 6G communication, and AI; advance major initiatives in e-mobility and biomedical devices with strong alumni support and collaborations; achieve multifold R&D expansion through challenge grants, promoting interdisciplinary national projects; position IIT (BHU) as a global leader in battery innovation via the new national facility; scale technology transfers and commercialization to double current levels (from 2 in 2024-25); establish dedicated centers for emerging technologies like a “Center for Semiconductor and AI Innovation” with industry ties.. 

**Entrepreneurship Scaling:** Incubate and graduate additional faculty/student startups, leveraging dedicated funding and collaborations for sustainable innovation growth; launch an alumni-funded accelerator program for 20+ ventures annually, focusing on e-mobility and med-tech, with 50% emphasis on women-led initiatives. 

**Outreach Expansion:** Finalize MoUs with Uttar Pradesh Mahayogi Gorakhnath AYUSH University and Mahayogi Gorakhnath University for tech support in Eastern UP’s education, healthcare, and agriculture, supporting the $1 trillion economy vision; advance the Outreach portfolio to enhance student placements (targeting further increases from 1,326 offers and 400 internships), professional training, and social development; establish regional innovation hubs in Eastern UP for tech transfer in agriculture/healthcare, involving students in rural challenges. 

**Sustainability and Societal Impact** : Integrate SDGs into curriculum and research, mandating related modules and dedicating 20% of challenge grants to environment/sustainability projects; expand digital infrastructure with a campuswide AI-driven learning management system for personalized education and virtual labs. 

**Alumni and Global Engagement:** Leverage the historic AASSII Board elections and U.S. visit outcomes to deepen alumni ties, secure more donations/partnerships for chairs, grants, scholarships, and AI/data science research; launch annual global alumni summits and mentorship apps targeting 25,000+ registrations. 

We’re committed to transforming our institute into a globally recognized entity that’s not only academically excellent but also socially responsible. As we step into this new era, we’re dedicated to fostering innovation, excellence, and societal impact. Our focus is on creating an environment that encourages creativity, critical thinking, and collaboration to address pressing global challenges. 

## **Our Core Values comprise** 

- Innovation: Embracing cutting-edge technologies and approaches to drive progress. 

- Excellence: Striving for academic and professional excellence in all endeavours. 

- Social Responsibility: Promoting inclusivity, diversity, and sustainability. 

- Global Competitiveness: Enhancing our international reputation and partnerships. 

- Social Impact: Addressing societal needs and contributing to national development. 

By embracing these values, we aim to create a vibrant ecosystem that nurtures talent, fosters innovation, and inspires positive change. We’re poised to make a meaningful difference in the world and invite all stakeholders to join us on this journey. 

11 

Annual Report 2024-25 

Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi

## **5. Academic Programmes and Award of Degrees** 

The Institute offers Ph.D. programmes in 19 departments (Department of Humanistic Studies established in 201516), M.Tech. programme in 13 streams/specializations, M.Pharm. programme in one stream/specialization (M.Tech. programme started in Decision Sciences and Engineering instead of Industrial Management from the session 2020-21), B.Tech. programmes in 10 engineering departments, Dual Degree (B.Tech. and M.Tech.) programmes in 14 engineering departments/schools/science departments, B.Arch. programme in 1 department (Department of Architecture, Planning and Design, established in 2019-20), besides a preparatory course for SC/ST/PwD students during the year under report. 

The Institute developed online registration portal, fee deposition portal as well as declaration of results etc. through the online mode. The registration portal started from the session 2014-15 and onwards in the Institute to facilitate the students. The fee deposition portal has been designed and institute fees is being continuously and successfully deposited in online mode by the students from even semester 2019-20 and onwards. After COVID-19 all academic programmes related to admission, verifications, registration, teaching etc. has been done through the online as well as physical mode. From the session 2023-24, an online fee remission portal has been designed and implemented at the undergraduate level to take the application for fee remission from UG students and approved online every odd semester before collection of fees at the fee portal. 

An academic section, examination unit and scholarship section are under the Dean (Academic Affairs) of the Institute. Three smart lecture theatre complexes have been established and equipped with LCD projector in each class rooms alongwith the backup of the electricity. The classes for the Institute core courses, HULM and Institute Open elective are being held centrally in the lecture theaters of the Institute. The Examination unit publishes the online application forms for the admission to PG programmes annually and for Ph.D. Programmes biannually every year. Online profile registration, add/drop courses, department-wise/subject-wise students list, grade submission, declaration of results, transcripts has keen automated and taken care by the examination unit. The Scholarship section administers the fellowships (Institute or other) of the students. Academic Section administers the works related to Ordinances, admissions process for B.Tech./B.Pharm./IDD through JEE(Advanced) and M.Sc. through JAM jointly conducted by the IITs, students leave, conducting semester examinations, preparation of academic calendar, class time-tables under supervision of Dean (Academic Affairs)/Associate Dean (Academic Affairs), UG/Core Courses. The office of the Dean (Academic Affairs) has totally automated for the Ph.D. submission as well as for the submission of grades on the portal, overload, physical registration also. 

## **Admissions 2024–2025** 

Candidates for admission to the 4-Year B.Tech., 5-Year B.Arch. and 5-Year Dual Degree programmes were selected through JEE(Advanced) and on the basis of the All India Rank. 2-Year M.Sc. programmes started from the session 2019-20 and onwards in the Department of Physics and Chemistry, the candidates were selected through JAM, jointly conducted by the IITs. 2-Year M.Tech./M.Pharm. programmes, candidates get admitted on the basis of GATE/ GPAT score. Quite a few candidates were also selected for the M.Tech. programme under the Sponsored and Q.I.P. programmes through interviews and/or written tests. Selection for the Ph.D. programmes was done through tests/ interviews, they must qualify the GATE or GPAT or UGC/CSIR-NET. To attract the foreign national students for studies in India, the Institute has also taken admission in PG and Ph.D. Programmes through the Study in India Portal as well as ASEAN Fellowship programmes conducted by the Govt. of India. The Institute also takes admission of foreign nationals for the PG and Ph.D. programs from the session 2021-22 onwards through the newly started A2A Scholarship Scheme under ICCR, Govt of India. 

22

# Student intake numbers for PC (5%) category has not been added in calculating total number of seats, as provision for the physically challenged candidate will be made from within the respective category. 

## **Convocation** 

The 13th Convocation was held on October 06, 2023. Dr. Samir V. Kamat, Secretary, Department of Defence R&D and Chairman, DRDO was delivered the convocation address. A total of 1908 various degrees were awarded in 12th Convocation of the Institute. During 12th Convocation, approximately 1342 candidates were received their degrees in person. The department-wise details of the degrees awarded are provided in Table.

## **6. Department of Architecture, Planning and Design** 

## **Year of Establishment:** 2019 

**Head of the Department:** Dr. Rabi Narayan Mohanty 

## **Brief Introduction of the Department including major research area and major infrastructure:** 

The Department of Architecture, Planning, and Design was established in 2019, which was also the institution’s centenary year. The Department is recognized by the Council of Architecture (COA) and is dedicated to fostering the next generation of architects, planners, and designers. The Department of Architecture, Planning, and Design at IIT (BHU) Varanasi has a well-established reputation for excellence in heritage conservation and has been designated as a National Expert Heritage Body (NEHB) by the National Monuments Authority (NMA) of the Ministry of Culture, Government of India. The Department has collaborated with local authorities such as Varanasi Smart City Limited (VSCL), the Varanasi Development Authority (VDA), the National Mission on Clean Ganga (NMCG), the National Institute of Urban Affairs (NIUA), Indian Knowledge System (IKS-AICTE), National Monuments Authority (NMA), Archaeological Survey of India (ASI), and heritage organizations such as ICOMOS India. “Creating Humane Architecture with Cultural Heritage and Innovative Technology” is the philosophy upon which the Department operates.

## **7. Department of Ceramic Engineering** 

## **Year of Establishment:** 1924 

**Head of the Department:** Dr. Ashutosh Kumar Dubey (w.e.f. 01.09.2023) 

## **Brief Introduction of the Department including major research area and major infrastructure:** 

The Department of Ceramic Engineering has celebrated its Centenary Year in 2024 by organizing series of seminars, symposia, alumni meets and a concluding conference. The first-ever department was established in pre-independentIndia and Asia to develop skilled human resources for various ceramic industries in the country. The founder of Banaras Hindu University, Bharat Ratna Mahamana Pandit Mandan Mohan Malviya Ji instituted two courses: Glass and Ceramic Technology, with the noble objective of advancing glass and ceramic science and technology in India. In the year 1956, these departments amalgamated to form the Department of Silicate Technology, offering a 4-Year degree course by injecting balanced engineering and scientific contents into its curriculum. However, again the Department of Silicate Technology renamed as the Department of Ceramic Engineering under Institute of Technology, Banaras Hindu University (IT-BHU) in the year of 1968. Then the IT-BHU transformed into Indian Institute of Technology (IIT (BHU)) in the year 2012 and currently, the Department of Ceramic Engineering belongs to IIT (BHU), Varanasi. In the IIT systems, this is the only department that is offering degree courses in Ceramic Engineering. The department remarkably leads to the nation building with a vast contribution towards academic excellence, innovative research, and industrial collaborations. 

## **Centenary Celebration** 

To commemorate its Centenary Year in 2024 (CeraShatam 2024), the Department of Ceramic Engineering, IIT(BHU) Varanasi successfully organized an International Conference on Frontiers in Ceramic Materials (ICFCM 2024) from 16ᵗʰ to 18ᵗʰ December 2024. This landmark centenary conference provided a unique platform for ceramists, materials scientists, academicians, and industry experts to connect and exchange ideas with renowned scientists from across the globe. 

An **alumni meeting** was also held on 16ᵗʰ December 2024, offering an opportunity for our alumni to reconnect, interact, and exchange ideas, while also cherishing and reliving their golden memories at IIT(BHU). Highlights of the Centenary Celebrations included: 

- **Inaugural Ceremony:** A grand opening graced by keynote speeches from distinguished alumni and eminent academicians. 

- **Alumni Reunion:** Heartwarming reconnections with old friends, complemented by fine dining and entertainment. 

- **Conference Sessions, Invited Talks, and Panel Discussions:** Engaging deliberations on cutting-edge topics by leading experts and thought leaders. 

The conference theme centred on exploring the frontiers of ceramic materials and their transformative role in addressing global challenges in technology, sustainability, and advanced manufacturing. Over three days, participants engaged in an extensive range of topics—from functional ceramics to the latest advancements in quantum materials and devices. 

## **Key theme areas covered were:** 

- Functional Ceramics 

- Biomaterials 

- Materials for Energy and the Environment 

- Magnetic, Spintronic, and Superconducting Materials 

- Electronic and Electrical Materials and Devices 

- Computational Materials Science 

- Quantum Materials and Devices 

- Ceramic Additive Manufacturing 

- Refractories and Ultra-High Temperature Ceramics 

- Structural Ceramics and Composite Materials 

- Glass and Glass-Ceramics 

48 

Annual Report 2024-25 

Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi 

**==> picture [34 x 35] intentionally omitted <==**

With over **160 distinguished speakers and presenters** , ICFCM 2024 showcased the diversity and depth of contemporary research in ceramic science and engineering, marking a memorable milestone in the department’s illustrious journey.

## **Teaching Programs** 

1. 4 Years B.Tech. Degree in Ceramic Engineering 

2. 5 Years Integrated Dual Degree (IDD) in Ceramic Engineering 

3. 2 Years M.Tech. Degree in Ceramic Engineering 

4. Ph. D. in Ceramic Engineering 

## **Major Research Areas** 

- Semiconductors, optoelectronic and quantum devices 

- Dielectrics and insulators 

- Functional materials and thin films 

- Composites and high temperature materials 

- Advanced solid-state materials 

- Biomedical and healthcare materials 

- Magnetic, plasmonics and spintronic materials 

- Nanostructured materials 

- Advanced glass and glass-ceramic materials

## **8. Department of Chemical Engineering & Technology** 

## **Year of Establishment:** 1921 

**Head of the Department:** Prof. Monoj Kumar Mondal w.e.f. 01.01.2023 

## **Brief Introduction of the Department including major research area and major infrastructure:** 

Department of Chemical Engineering and Technology (ChE) at IIT (BHU) Varanasi with a long-standing reputation for excellence, began its journey in 1921 as the Department of Industrial Chemistry. We offer an exceptional educational experience, cutting-edge research opportunities, diverse degree options, and a strong alumni network. We have evolved into the Department of Chemical Engineering and Technology, constantly updating our programs to deliver education in the latest areas of chemical engineering. 

At ChE@IIT (BHU) Varanasi, we are committed in providing the highest quality education and fostering an environment that stimulates the growth of our students. We strive for excellence in chemical engineering education, research, and socioeconomic impact. With dedicated faculty members and an ambitious student body, we are proud of the contributions our graduates make both within India and on the international stage, excelling in academia and industry. 

Our department is equipped with world-class experimental research facilities, supported by the esteemed ParamShivay Supercomputer, which ranks among the best in the country. This advanced infrastructure enables our students and faculty to pursue impactful research, attracting extramural funding, forging industry collaborations, and achieving highimpact publications and patents. 

To strengthen our expertise in emerging areas, we have expanded our faculty while maintaining our core strengths in fluid mechanics, reaction engineering, process design and intensification, thermodynamics, and molecular simulations. Additionally, we actively foster entrepreneurial activities among our students, resulting in numerous successful ventures supported by the department. 

As a member of ChE@IIT (BHU) Varanasi, one will have the opportunity to shape the future of chemical engineering education and research. In all of our programmes, B.Tech., M.Tech., or Ph.D., we offer a wide range of courses. Our department encompasses state-of-the-art research facilities, a well-equipped workshop, smart classrooms, an auditorium, and a library, making it one of the largest in the country. Moreover, as a DST-FIST Sponsored Department, we have established strong ties with industrial organizations, engaging in consultancy projects and industry-funded research. A vibrant and thriving ChE@IIT (BHU) Varanasi community is synonymous to IIT (BHU) Varanasi. 

## **Major area of Research:** 

Currently major areas of research in the department are Advanced Energy Solutions, Bioscience & Technology, Catalysis and Reaction Engineering, Pollution Control Technology, Nanoscience & Technology, Renewable Energy, Soft and Active Matters, and theory and Computation. The department has identified five major thrust areas for future research as Advanced Materials, Catalysis and Reactor Engineering, Energy, Environmental Sustainability, Multiscale Transport processes with emphasis on developing affordable solutions for the problems in the country such as drinking water, energy (harvesting, production and storage) and healthcare

## **9. Department of Civil Engineering** 

## **Year of Establishment: 1949** 

**Head of the Department: Prof. Sasankasekhar Mandal (w.e.f. 01/01/2023)** 

## **Brief Introduction of the Department including major research area and major infrastructure:** 

The Department of Civil Engineering at IIT (BHU), Varanasi, has a rich legacy that dates back to 1949 when it was established as the Department of Civil and Municipal Engineering under Banaras Engineering College (BENCO). With the formal sanction of the Visitor of the University, the Department received its official recognition in 1956, and the B.Sc. (Civil & Municipal) degree was acknowledged by the Government of India in 1958. In 1975, the department was rechristened as the Department of Civil Engineering, marking its evolution into a comprehensive academic and research hub. Over the decades, the Department has nurtured thousands of graduates who have contributed significantly to academia, industry, and government organizations, in India and abroad. Today, the Department offers undergraduate, postgraduate, and doctoral programmes, catering to a wide spectrum of specialized areas such as: 

- Structural Engineering 

- Geotechnical Engineering 

- Transportation Engineering 

- Hydraulics and Water Resources Engineering 

- Environmental Engineering 

- Engineering Geoscience 

- Geo-Informatics 

The Department has been at the forefront of collaborating with national agencies such as ANRF, CSIR, UGC, HUDCO, AICTE, and ministries such as Ministry of Road Transport and Highways, Ministry of Jal Nigam, etc., as well as with semigovernment and private organizations, to address pressing challenges in infrastructure development and sustainable urbanization. Its research programmes integrate advanced technical solutions with policy-relevant insights, enabling meaningful impact on society at large. The Department also emphasizes holistic student development. Through its Civil Engineering Society, it organizes lectures, workshops, group discussions, technical competitions, and cultural events. A highlight of the student calendar is Shilp, the annual fest of Civil Engineering, which combines technical innovation with creativity and cultural vibrancy. 

## **Major Research Areas** 

The Department is actively engaged in cutting-edge research across two broad groups: 

## **Group A: Structural, Geotechnical, Transportation Engineering & Engineering Geoscience** 

Thrust Areas: 

- Development and characterization of smart structures and materials 

- Construction technologies for sustainable infrastructure 

- Resilient and disaster-mitigating structures 

- Intelligent transportation systems and mobility planning 

## **Group B: Hydraulics & Water Resources, Environmental Engineering & Geo-Informatics** 

Thrust Areas: 

- River and watershed management 

- Water quality monitoring, treatment, and modelling 

- River health restoration and ecosystem resilience 

- Waste management, circular economy approaches, and pollution control 

- Remote sensing and GIS applications for environmental planning 

68 

Annual Report 2024-25 

Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi 

**==> picture [34 x 35] intentionally omitted <==**

## **Infrastructure** 

The Department is well equipped with modern infrastructure to support its academic and research activities: 

**==> picture [268 x 19] intentionally omitted <==**

**----- Start of picture text -----**<br>
S. No. Particulars Number<br>**----- End of picture text -----**<br>


|**S. No.**|**Particulars**|**Number**|
|---|---|---|
|1|Classrooms|10|
|2|Laboratories|10|
|3|Computers available for students|60|



In addition to these, the Department regularly upgrades its laboratories with advanced equipment and software tools to provide hands-on experience to students and researchers. This ensures that learning is not confined to classrooms but extends to experiential, project-based training and industry-oriented applications. 

## **Vision and Commitment** 

The Department of Civil Engineering at IIT (BHU) is committed to advancing sustainable, resilient, and socially inclusive infrastructure solutions for the 21st century. With a blend of strong foundational teaching, interdisciplinary research, and active industry collaboration, it seeks to produce graduates and research outcomes that will shape the future of civil engineering practice in India and beyond **.**

## **10. Department of Computer Science & Engineering** 

## **Year of Establishment:** 1983 

**Head of the Department:** Prof. Bhaskar Biswas w.e.f. 01.01.2025. 

## **Brief Introduction of the Department including major research area and major infrastructure:** 

The Department of Computer Science and Engineering was established in July 1983. The department offers a 4 year course, B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering, 5 year Integrated Dual Degree (B.Tech. and M.Tech.) in Computer Science and Engineering from 2005-2006, and Ph.D. degree in various specializations of Computer Science and Engineering. Computer Science and Engineering is the most sought- after branch for the JEE (Advanced) selected students that come to the Institute. Our graduates have distinguished themselves in higher studies at the top Universities. They also occupy positions of eminence in the computer industry. Our Alumni remain in constant touch with us and are contributing in the development of the department. Placements for our graduates are the best in the Institute. The faculty members of the department have international experience and training. The departmental research is focused in the areas of Artificial Intelligence, Parallel and Distributed Computing, Software Engineering, Image Processing and Computer Vision, Machine Learning\Deep Learning, Medical Image Processing, Pattern Recognition, Data mining and Web mining, semantic web, Natural Language Processing (NLP), IoT, Communications, Security and Information Extraction. The department has all the facilities to carry out the related teaching and research work.

## **11. Department of Electrical Engineering** 

## **Year of Establishment: 1919** 

## **Head of the Department: Prof. Rajeev Kumar Singh** 

## **Brief Introduction of the Department including major research area and major infrastructure:** 

Mahamana Pt. Madan Mohan Malviya founded BHU in the year 1916 with the benevolent and magnanimous contributions of the then Maharajas and other persons of eminence. The University was nurtured by Sir Sunderlal as the 1st V.C. of the University, followed by great visionaries, such as Pt. Madan Mohan Malviya, Acharya Narendradev, Sir S. Radhakrishnan and many other eminent personalities. The Benaras Engineering College (BENCO) was started in the year 1919, with its strong foundation laid by revered Prof. Charles A. King, Prof. H. P. Philpot and Prof. M. Sengupta. With the passage of time, the College of Mining and Metallurgy (MINMET) and the College of Technology (TECHNO) were included, expanding its horizon. These three colleges were merged and named as Institute of Technology in the year 1968 to give more autonomy for its better perspective in terms of academic and administrative decisions. It’s undergraduate students are admitted through Joint Entrance Examination (JEE) being conducted for all IITs. Since the inception of BENCO in 1919, a combined Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical and Electrical Engineering was awarded till 1952. The Department of Mechanical Engineering and the Department of Electrical Engineering were separated in 1953 and conferred separate degrees in respective disciplines. Presently, the Department of Electrical Engineering runs five postgraduate (M. Tech.) programmes in Electrical Machines and Drives (started in 1956), Power Systems (started in 1964), Control Systems (started in 1964), Power Electronics (started in 1982) and Interdisciplinary Systems Engineering (started in, 1982) and Ph. D. programme in all disciplines of Electrical Engineering. The department also has a five-year Integrated Dual Degree (IDD) Program (started in 2006) leading to a Master’s degree specialising in Power Electronics. The department has received the Special Assistance Programme (SAP) of UGC since 1988 and the COSIST program of UGC from 1995 to 2000. Apart from these, the department has been conducting research projects funded by DST, AICTE, CPRI and other R&D organisations of Govt. of India. The department has excellent placement records over the years. The students of this department are joining core companies such as PGCIL, IOCL, HPCL, Trident, Reliance, Maruti, etc. Electronics companies such as Broadcom, Sony, etc., regularly recruit students from this department. Our students regularly join software companies such as Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs, Citrix, Oracle, SISO, etc. The vast number of job offers is mainly due to the versatility of the branch, which ensures that students can sit for interviews in software, core electrical, electronic, and non-technical companies. Some of the department’s famous alumni include Mr. Nikesh Arora: Senior Vice President and Chief Business Officer at Google, Mr Rajiv Dogra: Indian diplomat, Ex- Consul General to Karachi, Pakistan, Mr. Gyanesh Pandey: Co-founder, CEO and CTO of Husk Power Systems and Mr Narla Tata Rao: Winner of Padma Shree, a doyen of the power sector in India. 

The department is pursuing academic activities with the following goals and objectives: 

- Further up-gradation and technological modernisation of infrastructural facilities. 

- Encouraging teaching innovations through audio-visual and multimedia aids. 

- Channelizing expertise of faculty in the frontier areas of electrical engineering. 

- Research, testing and consultancy. 

- Training the undergraduate and postgraduate students towards entrepreneurship in consonance with the liberalisation and privatisation policies of the Government. 

- Development of energy-efficient, environment-friendly electrical technologies as per the norms set by various planning, regulatory and other statutory bodies. 

## **Major Areas of Research** 

- Control System 

- Electrical Machines and Drives 

- Power Electronics 

- Power System 

- Systems Engineering

## **12. Department of Electronics Engineering** 

## **Year of Establishment: 1971** 

## **Head of the Department: Dr. Amritanshu Pandey w.e.f. 9th August, 2024** 

## **Brief Introduction of the Department/School:** 

The Department of Electronics Engineering came into existence as an offshoot of the Electrical Engineering Department in the year 1971 with a great effort from Prof. S.S. Banerjee. In the same year the erstwhile Banaras Engineering College (BENCO), College of Mining and Metallurgy and College of Technology were amalgamated to form the Institute of Technology-Banaras Hindu University (IT-BHU). The Department offers Bachelor, Master and Doctoral programs in Electronics Engineering with the major thrust areas of Microelectronics, Microwave Engineering, Digital Systems, Communication Systems and the newly introduced VLSI Architecture and Chip Design. The Department has been actively engaged in research since its inception as evidenced by the research publications. The first major financial support from the Department of Electronics (DoE), Govt. of India in the tune of Rs. 10 Crores was received by the Department in 1980 to carry out research for development of High Power Microwave Tubes. Subsequently, in recognition of excellent research contribution, the University Grants Commission (UGC) identified the Department to provide financial support under Special Assistance Program (SAP) in 1983 for five years. During this period, the department established three independent research centres e.g., Centre of Research in Microwave Tubes (CRMT), Centre for Research in Microelectronics (CRME) and Centre for Research in Microprocessor Applications (CRMA) supported by UGC/MHRD. The Department was further recognized as a Centre of Advanced Studies (CAS) by the UGC in 1989. The Department successfully completed three phases of CAS in the year 2009. The Department is also one of the DRDO centres for M.Tech. in Electronics Engineering. In addition to this, the Department has been actively pursuing manpower training and collaborative research programs in specialized areas to meet the national manpower requirement in R&D laboratories, academic institutions and industries. The Department has a close interaction with many reputed national R&D laboratories such as DRDO, ISRO, CSIR, Bharat Electronics Ltd. and leading industries as well as foreign universities. 

## **Major areas of Research** 

- Communication System Engineering 

- Digital System Engineering 

- RF and Microwave Engineering 

- Microelectronics Engineering 

## **Area of the Department/School (in square meters): 77.25 m x 46.10 m = 3561.22 m[2]**

## **Image:** 

**==> picture [270 x 232] intentionally omitted <==**

**Photoluminescence (PL) measurement setup** (F980) from Edinburgh Photonics, UK. This setup can provide PL from 200800 nm 

**==> picture [239 x 232] intentionally omitted <==**

**Vaccum evaporation and deposition systems** (FL 300 and BC 300) from Hind High vac, Bangalore, India. These setup works with e-beam evaporation (FL 300) and thermal evaporation (FL 300 and BC 300) for depositing thin films 

**==> picture [232 x 195] intentionally omitted <==**

An **‘’anechoic chamber’’** (“an-echoic” meaning nonreflective, non-echoing or echo-free) is a room designed to completely absorb reflections of either sound or electromagnetic waves. They are also often isolated from waves entering from their surroundings. **(Anechoic Chamber with 6 axis position for RF Characterization (up to 60 GHz))** 

**==> picture [133 x 195] intentionally omitted <==**

**==> picture [147 x 195] intentionally omitted <==**

**Handheld Vector Network Analyzer** with measurement facility up to 44 GHz. The microwave characterization of the device under test can be performed at any location as the instrument is portable in nature. 

112 

Annual Report 2024-25 

Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi

## **13.  Department of Mechanical Engineering** 

**Complete Name of Department: Mechanical Engineering Year of Establishment: 1919** 

**Head of the Department: Prof. Sandeep Kumar w.e.f 02/08/2024** 

## **Brief Introduction of the Department including major research area and major infrastructure:** 

The Department of Mechanical Engineering came into existence in 1919 under the leadership of Professor Charles A. King, the first Head of the Department and Principal of the erstwhile Banaras Engineering College. Over the last hundred years, the department has grown fourfold to become the largest department in IIT (BHU), Varanasi. The postgraduate and doctoral program in the department is well-established and infrastructural facilities exist for studies and research for a range of specialisations such as Machine Design, Thermal & Fluid Engineering, Production Engineering and Industrial Management. The ME curriculum emphasizes on developing industry-based engineers who contribute to the nation’s growth and command respect in society. A dedicated pool of faculty members helps students to learn in a technological and multidisciplinary environment. Advanced computational facilities and well-equipped laboratories aid in the motto of the department. The department encourages students to participate in various competitions related to technology and innovation. 

## **Major areas of research** 

Fracture mechanics, Vibrations and Dynamic Analysis, Machine Design, Tribology, Composite Materials, High-Temperature Wear and Lubrication, Surface Engineering, Computational Mechanics, FEM, MEMS, Smart Materials and Structures including Nanostructures, Structural Health Monitoring, Optical Fibre Sensing, Shape Memory Alloys, Heat Transfer, Multiphase Flow, Droplet Atomisation,  Bio-Fluid Dynamics, Fluid Machines, CFD, Porous-Media Flow, Microgravity Fluid Physics, Refrigeration, Air-conditioning, Alternate Fuels and Renewable Energy, Wind & Solar Energy, Climate Modelling, Microfluidics, Metal Forming/Joining, Machining, Micro-Machining, Welding engineering & Technology, 3D-4D-5D Printing & Additive Manufacturing, CAD-CAM, Robotics, Manufacturing Automation, Unconventional Manufacturing, Powder Metallurgy, Rapid Tooling, Metal Foams, Next-Generation Bio-Implants, Reverse Engineering, Design of Production Systems, Operations Research, Optimization, DOE, Logistics & Supply Chain Management, Agriculture 4.0, Location Science.
