## **27. Cultural Council** 

The Cultural Council of IIT (BHU) Varanasi celebrated a year of creativity, excellence, and vibrant cultural achievements. Under the guidance of  Dr.Akhilendra Pratap Singh, with the leadership of General Secretary Mr. Somil Mittal and Joint General Secretaries Mr. Rohit Kaushik and Mr. Harshit Mishra, the council actively contributed to the induction programme and orientations of all eight clubs, fostering inclusivity among freshers. Aagman’24, the intra-freshers competition, saw enthusiastic participation from over 821 students.The council organised celebrations like Janmashtami, Janjatiya Gaurav Divas, Constitution Day, and BharatiyaBhashaUtsav, alongside events such as a Charcoal Art Workshop by Nirdosh Singh, Le Début by the Literary Club, and collaborations like the Banaras Anime Quiz. Masquerades, the Dramatics Society, reinstated Abhivyakti in 2024 after a long post-Covid break. Collectively, council clubs secured 25+ awards at outstation competitions. The year also marked the launch of the first General Championship Culturals, with 850+ participants in 25+ events, and the 13[th] IITBHU MUN, which hosted 200+ delegates nationwide. In February 2025, the Literary Club, in collaboration with Kashiyatra’25, organised the IIT (BHU) PD Tournament, featuring 44 teams and 200+ participants, including 12 international teams. Kashiyatra’25, the Annual Socio-Cultural Fest, themed “SanskritiPravah”, received endorsements from renowned artists. The inauguration was graced by SPIC MACAY maestros Pt. Vishwa Mohan Bhatt and VidushiGeetaChandran, with electrifying pronites by Nakash Aziz and JubinNautiyal. 

## **Inter IIT** 

IIT (BHU) secured 8[th] place at the Inter IIT Cultural Meet 7.0 hosted by IIT Patna, achieving 7 positions across various events among 23 participating IITs. 

## **Science & Technology Council** 

Science and Technology Council The Science and Technology Council (SNTC) at IIT (BHU) Varanasi continued its tradition of excellence with several notable achievements. At Inter IIT Tech Meet 13.0, the Institute won 3 medals, including 2 gold and 1 bronze. The Robotics Club also advanced to the national finals of DD Robocon. The Council pushed forward innovations, such as UAV systems that qualified for ISRO’s IRUCO 2025. It successfully hosted Technex 2025, Asia’s oldest techno-management festival, which attracted wide participation from across the country. Additionally, the CyberSec vertical of COPS organized Kashi CTF, the Institute’s first international Capture the Flag event. This event drew over 3,000 participants from around the world and led to IIT (BHU) achieving its first-ever 1st place finish in an international-rated cyber security competition, SpookyCTF 2024. 

## **Social Service Council** 

The Social Service Council successfully conducted a diverse series of impactful initiatives and events. These included Crack It, a four-day career guidance program; the ABLT Lotus Pond Rejuvenation Project; Letter to Soldiers; and Alumni Archives, a video interview series featuring distinguished SSC alumni. The Council also organized the Kashi Utkarsh Club’s Medical Camp and Sangharsh, an inter-basti sports event; the Sahyog Club’s Gram Mela; as well as the Social Project Club’s Abhipraya and industrial visits. Institute’s flagship socio-awareness festival, Jagriti 2025, was organized on the theme “Architects of Sapience”. It saw active participation from individuals representing colleges across India and featured thought-provoking discussions and guest talks by eminent personalities including Dr. Tanu Jain, Neha Agrawal, Lt. Gen. D. P. Pandey, among others. 

## **Film & Media Council** 

The Film & Media Council brings digital storytelling to life with hands-on workshops, photo walks, media and video walks, design marathons, and animation events. The council bagged the Bronze Cup for overall Digital Arts at Inter IIT Cultural Meet 7.0 with a Bronze in Animation, Gold in Online Photo story, Bronze in Street Photography, and a 4th-place in Photoshop Battle. Every year the council hosts India’s largest Digital Arts Fest, featuring workshops by creators like Doodlenath, Shubham Sharma, and Joey. The fest concludes with a vibrant Dandiya Night, celebrating a unique blend of cultural tradition and digital creativity. The Games and Sports Council conducted a series of events, including the Intra-Freshers. 

Annual Report 2024-25 275 

Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi 

**==> picture [34 x 35] intentionally omitted <==**

## **Games & Sports Council** 

The Games and Sports Council conducted a series of events, including the Intra-Fresher’s tournament, National Sports Day, General Championship, Inter-hostel Annual Athletics meet, Swachata Run, and Summer Camp for the various sports in the council. The flagship events, Saturnalia and Spardha 2024, were organized successfully, with a footfall of 5000 and 2500, respectively, all over the country. In a bid to foster inclusivity and celebrate women’s participation, the Council also organized the Girls Weekend — a week-long festival of athleticism, empowerment, and pride. Our sports contingent has brought us immense pride this year with a truly remarkable. 

Performance at the Inter-IIT Sports Meet, jointly organized by IIT Kanpur and IIT Indore. From our earlier standing of 12th, we have risen to secure the 7th overall position among all 23 participating IITs — a leap that reflects the determination, grit, and indomitable spirit of our students. Among the many shining moments, we proudly celebrate the Gold and Silver in Volleyball (Men and Women, respectively); a Gold in Basketball; a Silver in Cricket; and 1 Silver, 1 Bronze, and a Fourth position in Weightlifting. Our Aquatics team made waves with 2 Silver and 2 Bronze medals, along with an inspiring 2nd place in March Past, a true reflection of our coordination, discipline, and collective pride. This year also marked a milestone in our sports identity with the launch of our official mascot — The Blue Bull — under whose banner all IIT (BHU) athletes will now march and compete, symbolizing strength, resilience, and unity. 

These victories are more than sporting success. They embody the values of IIT (BHU) — 

Resilience, teamwork, and the spirit of never giving up. To our graduating students, I say: carry this same spirit with you. Wherever life takes you, let the Blue Bull within inspire you to aim higher, push harder, and rise stronger. 

276 

Annual Report 2024-25 

Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi 

**==> picture [34 x 35] intentionally omitted <==**

## **28. Training and Placement Cell, IIT(BHU)** 

IIT (BHU) Varanasi’s Training and Placement Cell acts as a bridge between ambitious students and the world of thriving careers. This year witnessed a phenomenal turnout, with a record-breaking 398 companies visiting campus to hold campus interviews and made an average CTC of INR 24.56 LPA **.** The number of companies visiting the campus has increased by ~ 13.06% compared to last academic year i.e. 2023-24. The highest package offered this year was 2.20 Cr. INR by Databricks. This diverse group of recruiters offered a symphony of exciting opportunities across various industries. From the foundation of core engineering to the dynamic realm of IT and beyond, students explored possibilities in finance, cutting-edge research and development, and strategic consulting firms. This impressive display showcased the vast range of career paths available to IIT (BHU) Varanasi graduates. 

The institute’s commitment to excellence extends beyond placements. The Training and Placement Cell actively fosters international exposure for deserving students. This year, Databricks extended their reach beyond geographical boundaries, offering coveted international opportunities. This surge in international placements is a testament to the institute’s growing reputation and the caliber of its graduates who are increasingly sought after by leading corporations worldwide. 

Google, Microsoft, Oracle, Intuit, Sciforn Solutions, Sprinklr, Pacestock Broking, Axxela, AQR CAPITAL, Siemens EDA, Cohesity, D.E. Shaw, Wells Fargo, Zomato, Flipkart, Decimal Point, Nvidia, Cars 24, Standard Chartered, Sedemac, Arista, Vmock, Meesho, Texas Instruments, Mastercard, Winzo, Salesforce, Deutsche Bank, MyKaarma, VISA, Future First, JPMC, Dev Rev, Walmart, Eightfold, A.P.T. Portfolio, NK Securities, Goldman Sachs, Cisco, BNY Mellon, Axtria, Harness, UiPath, ANZ Bank, Qualcomm, Accordion, ICICI Bank and Uber visited our campus for full-time recruitment. The Placement Cell goes a step further by facilitating a robust internship program. 

The Placement Cell continues to strengthen career opportunities through a robust internship program. In the 2024–25 session, 502 students secured paid internships including one international internship offer, with the highest stipend touching ₹5 lakhs per month and an average stipend of INR 1.04 Lakh per month. These internships provide invaluable real-world exposure, allowing students to explore diverse fields, refine their skills, and build a strong foundation for their future careers. Notably, there has been a steady rise in companies seeking talent with specialized expertise in quantitative analysis **,** consulting, and core engineering. This trend underscores the institute’s commitment to delivering a well-rounded education that aligns with the evolving demands of the job market. 

**Improvement in placement during the last three years is shown in the figure below: -** 

**==> picture [185 x 153] intentionally omitted <==**

**==> picture [152 x 153] intentionally omitted <==**

The Training and Placement Cell doesn’t just connect students with jobs; it also collaborates with leading organizations and institutes to create internship and training programs. This gives students valuable real-world experience before they even graduate. Additionally, the Cell has taken the initiative by running three successful editions of the Career Development Programme (CDP) in 2023, 2024 and 2025. This program specifically focuses on enhancing students’ preparedness for their future careers. In last edition its attracted a lot of interest, hosting over 20 companies for more than 25 events across six sought-after profiles, including Quantitative Finance, Software Development, Data Science, Core Engineering, Analytics and Product Management. Over 3,500 students have registered to participate in workshops, networking sessions, and even hackathons led by renowned industry professionals. These events provide students with practical skills and industry insights, giving them a competitive edge in the job market. 

Annual Report 2024-25 277 

Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi

## **29. Resource & Alumni, IIT (BHU) Varanasi** 

Dean, Resource and Alumni Prof. Hiralal Pramanik -(w.e.f. 01.04.2024-31.03.2025) 

## **1. Introduction:** 

The Resource & Alumni office of the Institute works for the functions as delineated by the Director of the Institute (Vide letter No. IIT (BHU)/2014-15/504/L Dated 9[th] September 2014 and Subsequent modification. The following works/functions are carried out as: 

- I. Alumni Processes and Functions [through dedicated office and Student Alumni Interaction Cell (SAIC)] 

- II. Gandhi Technology Alumni Centre-Guest Houses. (Through Coordinator, GTAC). 

- III. Alumni Interactions: Coordinating with alumni at regional, national, and international level for overall development of the Institute. Identifying and recognizing the alumni and organizing alumni reunions with the help of alumni. 

- IV. Seeking and Raising Donations and Endowments for student scholarship/ awards, medals, Faculty chairs and facility development. 

- V. Newer Dimensions. 

## **2. Objectives** _**:**_ 

Developing effective mechanisms of communication through all alumni by creating complete databases, developing and using tools and technologies, websites, portals and keeping them updated with their Alma matters. 

_Some ongoing activities related to above:_ 

---Alumni Registration portal developed for registration of alumni and collecting their contact details for enriching the database for effective communication. 19,499+ Alumni have already registered on the portal. 

---Alumni Newsletter: Being published since January-2021 and communicated monthly to all alumni groups. Keeping all the alumni updated with their Alma matters through Alumni Newsletters and posting the information on website and social media platforms on regular basis 

---Regular Communications to alumni through Group Email IDs (~25000), Institute Website, Alumni Website, and Social Media Platforms (LinkedIn, Facebook, Twitter, Instagram etc.). 

- ~ Honoring the alumni through distinguished alumnus awards and facilitating them at various occasions. Eight (08) alumni were awarded distinguished alumnus award in different categories during 2024-25. 

- ~ Organizing regular meetings/ reunions etc. 

- ~ Engaging the alumni for overall development of the Institute fraternity through intellectual talks, seminars, workshops, online classes etc. 

- ~ Interacting with alumni groups for: 

--Creation of Scholarships, endowment funds, angel funds etc. 

--Creation of Alumni funded Institute Chair Positions in various Depts./ Schools/ Centers. 

--Exploring the possibility for minor/ major donations for Infrastructural development, development of centers, schools, facility etc. 

## **3. Alumni Association of IIT (BHU) (AASSII), Varanasi** 

## **Introduction** 

The Alumni Association of IIT BHU Varanasi (AASSII) continues to strengthen the bond between the alma mater and its alumni across the globe. This report presents a summary of key events, initiative and achievements during the past year, reflecting our shared commitment to community building, institutional support and alumni welfare. 

## **Annual General Meeting (AGM)** 

The Annual General Meeting of the Alumni Association of IIT BHU Varanasi was held successfully on 28th November 2024 at IIT (BHU) Varanasi with active participation of members across various chapters. The AGM focused on reviewing the year’s progress and key developments. 

278 

Annual Report 2024-25 

Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi 

**==> picture [34 x 35] intentionally omitted <==**

## **AASSII Board Election process** 

The Board of Directors of Alumni Association of IIT (BHU) Varanasi, in its meeting on 2[nd] September 2024, had resolved to form an elected body and requested Prof. Amit Patra, Patron to constitute Nomination and Election Committee (NEC). Prof. Amit Patra, Director, IIT (BHU)Varanasi and Patron has nominated Prof. Hiralal Pramanik, Dean (Resource and Alumni) IIT (BHU) Varanasi as an Election Officer and Chairman of NEC and Dr. Kishor P. Sarawadekar, Associate Professor, Department of Electronics Engineering, Dr. Pramod Soni, Assistant Professor, Department of Civil Engineering, Dr. Nitai C. Maji, Assistant Professor, Department of Chemical Engineering & Technology, Dr. Akhilendra Pratap Singh, Assistant Professor, Department of Mechanical Engineering and Dr. Ramji Agrawal as Members of NEC 

The Nomination and Election Committee has conducted Election process very smoothly in a transparent manner and the Election result was announced during AGBM of AASSII. The new Board of Directors were constituted for the period 2024-26. Mr. Nitin Malhotra has been elected President of AASSII. 

## **Aligning with existing Chapter / Association** 

The Benares Alumni Association of IIT(BHU) Varanasi (BAA), Association of IIT (BHU) Alumni, New Delhi (AIBA), BHU Engineers Alumni, Lucknow (BEAL), IIT BHU Alumni Association of Western India, IIT BHU Global Alumni Association (IBGAA) have been aligned with AASSII. The IBGAA, USA is having their constitution, regulated by US laws. Therefore, the aligning of IBGAA is being taken up later on. 

## **QS Ranking of IIT (BHU) Varanasi for 2024** 

The QS World University Rankings is a portfolio of comparative college. The Alumni Association of IIT (BHU) Varanasi has made strategic efforts to improve the QS Ranking of IIT(BHU) Varanasi in 2024. The consistent efforts of AASSII have resulted improved QS Ranking of IIT (BHU) Varanasi from 651 to 571 and among IITs 17 to 12 in India. 

## **Health insurance plan for Alumni and their family members** 

The Alumni Association of IIT (BHU) Varanasi has offered economical Health Insurance Plan for members, their family members, their parents or parent in laws at very competitive rates in 2022. In view of the overwhelming response, the Health Insurance is continued in 2023, 2024 and also in 2025. The Health Insurance scheme is very popular among the Alumni. 

## **Economical and beneficial Travel for Alumni and their family members** 

The Alumni Association of IIT (BHU) Varanasi is continuing its agreement with Make My Trip (MMT) to facilitate economical and beneficial travel for Alumni. The MMT is offering special corporate fare, reduced / nil cancellation, nil convenience fees etc. 

## **Participation in IIT (BHU) Varanasi programme** 

The Office bearers and members of Alumni Association of IIT (BHU) Varanasi have actively participated in the Annual Convocation and other events of the Institute. They are also supporting the annual events Technex & Institute Day, Kashiyatra. 

## **Frontier in Ceramic Materials & Alumni Meet Centenary Celebration-2024** 

The Department of Ceramic Engineering, IIT (BHU) Varanasi has organised three days International Conference on “Frontier in Ceramic Materials & Alumni Meet Centenary Celebration- 2024” from 16th -18th December 2024 at Swatantrata Bhawan, BHU, Varanasi. The programme was actively participated by our Alumni across the globe. President Shri Nitin Malhotra and other alumni have actively participated in the programme. 

## **Participation in PAN IIT programme** 

The Pan IIT Alumni India is an umbrella organization, representing alumni of all Indian Institute of Technology (IITs). Office bearers and members of Alumni Association of IIT(BHU) Varanasi are actively participating in their programmes. The PanIIT has organized “PanIIT World of Technology Global Summit-PIWOT-2025” from 17th to 19th January 2025 at Jio World Convention Centre, Mumbai. Office bearers and members of AASSII have not only participated in the programme but also played key role in organizing the event. IIT (BHU) Varanasi has also displayed its activities under the leadership of Honourable Director, IIT (BHU) Prof. Amit Patra and Prof. Hiralal Pramanik, Dean (Resource and Alumni). 

Annual Report 2024-25 279 

Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi 

**==> picture [34 x 35] intentionally omitted <==**

## **Event by other Chapters** 

The present Chapters and Associates located at USA, New Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru, Chennai, Lucknow and Varanasi and members at Pune, Hyderabad, Ahmedabad, Kolkata are organizing social, cultural and academic activities to maintain bonding of their members. Bengaluru Chapter has organised “Future of Data, AI and Applications” on 7th December 2024 and “Future of SDE and PM in the Era of Gen AI” on 8th March 2025 at KDEM Office, HSR Layout, Bengaluru. The programme was actively participated by large number of Alumni. The festive events on the occasions of Deepawali and Holi are participated by members and their family members. Some of the events are organized in virtual mode which attracts participation by other chapters also. The Chapters and Associates are also organizing their Annual General Meeting. 

## **Connect and Learn** 

The “Connect and Learn” is a very popular programme among alumni. In the programme, the distinguished alumni, from diverse professional path, come together to share real-world experiences, career insights and practical advice with participants. The discussions spanned Industries, challenges, personal growth and strategies that help them navigate transition from academic to a workplace and from early role to leadership. Alumni based at New Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru, Chennai, Lucknow, Varanasi, Pune, Hyderabad, Ahmedabad, Kolkata have successfully organized “Connect and Learn” programme. 

Prof. Amit Patra, Director, IIT (BHU) Varanasi was Chief Guest in the “Connect and Learn” programme organized by Association of IIT (BHU) Alumni, New Delhi (AIBA) on 6th July 2024 at PHD House New Delhi. 

## **Alumni Registration and Life Membership:** 

Alumni are encouraged to register with Alumni Registration Portal at https://alumni-reg.iitbhu.ac.in and become Life Members at the Link https://forms.gle/jsdudpTbfKgmbYfs8 The subscription for Life membership is Rs. 4,000.00. Alumni who have passed out during the last three years can become Life member at reduced subscription of Rs. 2,500.00 only. The Life Members enjoy a varied array of schemes which benefit them and their families directly. Members are requested to take Life membership and avail benefits. 

The AASSII Life membership to Final year students are offered at subsidised rate at Rs. 2,500.00. 

## **Membership Card** 

The Alumni Association of IIT (BHU) Varanasi is issuing Membership Card to all Life Members who submit their personal details in the required format and submit to AASSII online. The membership card gives the unique Membership Number allotted to Alumni along with Photograph, Batch Year, Branch, Residential address, Contact number and Blood Group. 

## **AASSII details at a glance** 

## **AASSII Office** 

The Management of IIT (BHU) Varanasi has allotted office for AASSII which is adjacent to the Chamber of Dean (Resources & Alumni). AASSII’s office actively participates and helps in organizing various reunions at the Institute campus. It is nodal centre for Alumni to cater their requirements. It supports the AASSII in the role of front office as well as back office. AASSII is registered under Section 80G and 12A of the Income Tax. 

**Website:** Website link of Alumni Association of IIT (BHU, Varanasi is https://alumni.iitbhu.ac.in/. The AASSII website is integrated with Website of IIT (BHU), Varanasi. 

## **4. IIT (BHU) Foundation, USA** 

IIT (BHU) Foundation, a non-profit body based in Albany, New York, USA, was a specific response to the need for a capital campaign to support IIT (BHU). Started by our highly passionate alumni to enable IIT (BHU)’s transformation into a trendsetting pioneer of technical education over the next century. Through the benevolence of alumni donor network contemporized with the strategic plans of their beloved alma mater, the Foundation seeks to foster the development of the Institute by capital raised through generous gifts, bequests, grants, and donations of the alumni. 

## **5. Student Alumni Interaction Cell (SAIC)** 

_**Student Alumni Interaction Cell (SAIC)**_ is dedicated to fostering threefold interaction among students, alumni, and the Institute, creating a vibrant community that provides opportunities to thrive for the benefit of the commonwealth. 

280 

Annual Report 2024-25 

Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi 

**==> picture [34 x 35] intentionally omitted <==**

Under the guidance of the **Dean (Resource and Alumni)** , Prof. Hiralal Pramanik (Jan ‘24- current), the cell further strengthened its alumni relations. The new SAIC team for session 2024-2025 was formed in June 2024. 

The **Alumni Visiting Faculty (AVF)** program continued in hybrid mode. The program witnessed renowned alumni taking full-credit courses as visiting faculty, with a total of 9 alumni teaching 4 courses in the odd semester and 2 courses in the even semester. The courses received enrolment from 950+ students for the academic year 2024-25. The **AlumniGuided Mock Interviews** were conducted across 2 drives for the intern and the placement season. It saw participation from 900+ students and 220+ alumni. A total of 900+ interviews were conducted in both drives for 8+ domains. SAIC enhanced its **Social Media** presence among alumni and students through 11 initiatives, including a collaborative video with the Cine Club, 10 Instagram reels with 50k+ views, 10 Alumni Achievement posts, and the release of “SAIC Wrapped” and “Hostel Diaries,” also initiating the ‘Know Your Alumni’ series along with “Reunion Diaries”. 

Under the **Beyond the Degree** initiative, SAIC collaborated with the Electronics Engineering Society, the Club of Programmers, The Business Club, the Research Cell, and more to organize more than 11 informative talks and workshops. The cell also organized the second edition of SARC TANK, a Pan IIT series of competitions in collaboration with the Alumni Cell, IIT Bombay. Volunteers from SAIC organized a booth during the 13th Convocation Ceremony, engaging the graduands in creative activities such as ‘Wall of 2024’, ‘Nostalgic Notes,’ ‘Last Note To,’ and a photo booth for the 2024 graduates who became the alumni of IIT (BHU). SAIC’s registration booth at the ceremony also motivated graduands to register themselves on the official Alumni Registration Portal. 

- _**I. Alumni Connect**_ **:** With the mission to strengthen the bond between the alumni and the students, frequent engaging sessions and formal/informal meets were organized both online and in person. 

The annual **Student Alumni Mentorship Program** , aimed at providing personalized guidance and insights to students from the alumni, witnessed registrations from 450+ students who received one-on-one guidance from 80+ alumni mentors across 12 diverse domains. We also organized the **Ask The Alumni** initiative as part of the previous program, which received registrations from 320+ students and around 70+ alumni. This initiative is specially curated for 1styear students, targeting their holistic development. 

**Connect & Learn - the Student Alumni Meet-up** saw an increase in participation from both students and alumni this year. We received 250+ student registrations and 70+ alumni registrations this year across 8 cities of the country, including Bangalore and Delhi. 

To update the alumni community about the latest developments at the Institute, Student Alumni Interaction Cell (SAIC) released 12 editions of its monthly alumni newsletter- **Alma Communiqué** , from April 2024. Covering information from different sections of all the realms- students, alumni, and the Institute, the newsletter had an overall readership of 4000+ alumni from across the globe. 

**SAIC’s website** ( _saic.iitbhu.ac.in_ ) hosted 14,000+ users and touched a total of 51,000+ page views. Apart from increasing the awareness of the **Alumni Registration Portal** through its social media platforms, SAIC also converted its database to add 6,300+ members to the portal. The current registration stands at 19,500+. Moving forward, SAIC aims to build more alumni connections and closely knit the Institute’s vast alumni network together. The UI/UX design and the front-end development of the new SAIC website have been completed. The back-end development is in process. 

- _**I. Alumni Reunions/ Meetups**_ **:** The Institute witnessed 11 mesmerizing reunions, bringing families of 550+ alumni together to reminisce fond memories whilst establishing connection with the Institute.

## **30. Gandhi Technology Alumni Centre** 

Gandhi Technology Alumni Centre (GTAC) has been established in 2007 with the help of alumni’s of IIT(BHU) for the purpose of providing stay to guests which includes alumni’s, employees, students etc. Since then it has been a long journey till now. 

## **Coordinator, Prof. Meghanshu Vashista** 

## **Facilities** 

## **Old Building:** 

There are total of 72 rooms, which includes 4 suites and 68 rooms. All rooms are air conditioned and have facilities like TV, telephone, two beds, table and chairs etc. 

## **GTAC Extension (New Building):** 

There are 44 standard room, 04 Suites room and 04 Executive Suite rooms. All rooms are well furnished and facilities like TV, air-conditioner, chair & tables etc. 

## **Waiting Hall** 

A waiting room for the guest which also serves purpose of meeting room in required condition. 

## **Dining Facilities** 

For dining facilities to In-house guests. Provides breakfast, lunch, dinner, tea, snacks etc. to the In-house guest and provide catering facilities to the institute departments/units whenever required. 

## **Conference room:** 

For conferences, meeting, seminars etc. for the students or faculties of the Institute. 

**Activities:** GTAC provides meetings and ensure comfortable stay for Guest. 

## **Images:**

## **31. Research and Development Activities** 

## **Dean: Professor Rajesh Kumar.** 

**Introduction:** Institute has a mission to fulfil the needs of the nation through Research and Innovation. Faculty members and students are engaged in cutting edge research under various schemes. To inculcate research culture in the students, the institute has set up Tinkering Labs in various departments. Students are involved in research projects from almost the early stage of their education. The institute gives partial support to the research initiatives of faculty members through grants like Seed Money, Research Support Grant, and R & D Thrust Area Grants. 

The institute has also provided Lab Grants for up-gradation of teaching labs and supports Central Instrument Facility acquisitions. Faculty members of the institute are active in frontier areas of research, and Govt. research sponsoring agencies and many reputed industries have supported their efforts. A tremendous initiative of IIT (BHU) Varanasi, green and sustainable technology initiative, aims at promoting interdisciplinary research with industry collaboration for developing indigenous green, clean, and sustainable technologies that may be cost-effective, fulfil the local needs, and can be scalable to a global scale. The initiative aims to achieve excellence in research and capacity building on green & sustainable technologies and related applications, and leverage these technologies for the benefit of India. 

The institute is also interested in expanding collaboration with renowned foreign universities and Institutions to enhance the exchange of research activities. IIT(BHU) Varanasi since its conversion in an IIT entered 56 numbers of International MoU’s with various Institute and now 25 MoU’s are active. IIT(BHU) Varanasi has developed a research and innovation friendly environment supported through state of art infrastructure at Department/School level. In IIT(BHU) Varanasi, total numbers of on-going project (including Institute projects) is 406 till F.Y 2023-24 amounting to Rs. 147,68,52,000/including 11 Internationally funded project of amount Rs. 2,30,69,000/-. Further, there are 45 schemes running in the Institute with a total cost of ~436 crores. Total number of new projects sanctioned is 91 amounting to ~27.17 crores, 32 patents were filed and 49 patents were awarded in F.Y. 2022-23. In the last F.Y. 2023-24, total number of new projects increased to 93, 46 patents were filed and 66 patents were awarded. 

The lists of new and on-going projects as well as consultancy/testing projects are shown below:

## **32. IDEATION INNOVATION & INCUBATION (I-3) FOUNDATION** 

## **Year of Establishment: 2021** 

## **Name of Directors: Director, IIT (BHU), Prof. Rajnesh Tyagi, Prof. Manoj Kumar Meshram, Prof. Rajesh Kumar Upadhyay** 

Ideation Innovation & Incubation (I-3) Foundation, namely _I-3 Foundation_ is an umbrella organization at IIT (BHU), Varanasi for fostering entrepreneurial ecosystem and nurturing start-ups. It administers various units which provide ‘Start to Scale’ support for entrepreneurship and facilitates research activities to convert into commercial ventures. I-3 Foundation provides start to end incubation and acceleration support for startups in domains like Agriculture & applied sector, AI/ML/ IoT based, Clean tech, Water tech, E-Commerce, New and Renewable Energy and Environment Sustainability etc. 

## **Activities (1[st] April 2024 – 31[st] March 2025)** 

1. RKVY-RAFTAAR Agri Business Incubator: Rashtriya Krishi Vikas Yojna - Remunerative Approaches For agriculTure And Allied sector Rejuvenation (RKVY-RAFTAAR) Agri Business Incubator (R-ABI) is operational at IIT (BHU) Varanasi since 2019. It is funded by Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare (MoA&FW), Govt. of India. It is aimed at promoting agri-preneurship and agribusiness by providing financial support and nurturing the incubation ecosystem. 

   - On 10th April 2024, we successfully launched Cohort-7, encompassing all applications from promising agricultural startups. 

   - Following the initiation of Cohort-7’s application process, we garnered 119 submissions for the pre-seed (ANKURAN) program. In the initial screening phase, 19 applications were selected and invited for Agripreneurship Orientation Program (AOP). Additionally, the seed (PRASFUTAN) program received 213 applications, with 23 of them making it through the first round of screening and being invited for the AOP. 

   - The Agripreneurship Orientation Program took place from 12th September 2024. The one-month program, totaling forty-five hours, included sessions covering business, technical, and legal aspects. These sessions aimed to offer participants essential insights and knowledge for the initiation of their agricultural startups. 

   - The 1st RIC meeting for Cohort-7 took place on November 27th and 28th, 2024. A total of 42 startups presented their ideas during online session. Following careful consideration, the RIC members shortlisted 18 startups in Pre-Seed Stage and 7 startups in Seed Stage for the Selection & Investment Committee (SIC). 

   - The Selection & Investment Committee (SIC) Evaluation Meeting of Cohort-7 for recommended start-ups was held on 15th January 2025. The meeting was organized by Pusa Krishi, ZTM & BPD Unit, ICAR-IARI, in which 24 start-ups (7 Prasfutan and 17 Ankuran) pitched their idea and 9 promising start-ups were selected for grantin-aid. 

   - The 3rd and 4th RIC Meeting of 10 startups of Cohort-1, 3, 4, 5 and 6 was held on 28th November 2024. Startups shared their progress with the committee. 

   - Start-ups were invited to apply under COHORT-7 of RKVY-RAFTAAR Agri Business Incubator (R-ABI) on 10th April, 2024 through digital and print media. 

   - Master class series for selected agri based startups in the year 2023-24, was organized by Pusa Krishi, New Delhi between 1st April to 12th April 2024. The newly selected six (6) seed stage and three (3) pre-seed stage start-ups of 2023-24 (COHORT-6), R-ABI also attended the sessions. 

   - Dr. Abhishek Singh, Business Manager, R-ABI, IIT(BHU), delivered a lecture and sensitization on the topic of “Innovation & Agri-preneurship” to the participants of IDE Bootcamp organized under the aegis of IIC, Ministry of Education on 2nd May 2024 at E-Hall, IIT (BHU) Varanasi. 

   - Prof. Amit Patra, Hon’ble Director, IIT (BHU) Varanasi, visited the Incubation Centre on 8th August 2024. He interacted with start-ups and appreciated their work. 

   - The Agripreneurship Orientation Program (AOP) for the shortlisted start-ups of COHORT-7, was started on 12th September 2024 and it was concluded on 15th October 2024. 

376 

Annual Report 2024-25 

Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi 

**==> picture [34 x 35] intentionally omitted <==**

   - The Selection and investment committee (SIC) meeting of 24 start-ups of COHORT-7 was organized by Pusa Krishi, New Delhi on 15th January, 2025. After a detailed presentation, the committee selected 9 start-ups (3 – Seed, 6 – Pre-seed) for the of Rs. 105 Lakhs. 

   - Mr. D. P. Singh Duhun, founder of M/s Unnat Krishi Evam Herbal Techno-solution Pvt. Ltd. was awarded by ICAR-IARI, New Delhi on 6th June 2024. 

   - M/s Agrifeeder Pvt. Ltd has been awarded as the upcoming agritech startup of 2024 Wolf den Investor Summit, Mumbai on 22nd June 2024. 

   - Mr. Ashok Manwani, founder of M/s Manwani’s Innovative Pearl Culture LLP was awarded by the Jagran Agri Panchayat Award 2024. Hon’ble Agriculture Minister Mr. Shivraj Singh Chauhan facilitated him by the Award and Certificate on 27th August 2024 at Hotel Ashoka, New Delhi. 

   - Our startup M/s Allywing Solutions Pvt. Ltd. participated in 13th National Seed Conference organized at International Rice Research Institute (IRRI), Varanasi from 28th to 30th November 2024. 

   - Hon’ble Chief Minister of Uttar Pradesh, Yogi Aaditya Nath Ji gave commendation certificate to Dr. Kamini Singh, one of our agri startups founders of M/s JVKS Agrotech Pvt. Ltd. (now M/s Doctor Moringa Pvt. Ltd.) 

   - ASSOCHAM organized “Uttar Pradesh AGRO Pradarshini” from 17th to 20th December at Indira Gandhi Pratishthan, Lucknow. Our agri startups M/s Navariti Innovations Pvt. Ltd. from Hyderabad participated in the event and got good response from various stakeholders. 

   - Our start-up, M/s Navariti Innovation Pvt. Ltd. participated in  International Conference on Rainfed Agriculture at ICAR-Central Research Institute for Dryland Agriculture (CRIDA), Hyderabad which is being organized from 29th to 31st January 2025 at Hyderabad. The inaugural function of the International Conference was held on Wednesday, January 29, 2025. 

   - M/s Navariti Innovation Pvt. Ltd. also participated in “IInvenTiv 2025” organized from 28th Feb to 1st  March 2025 at IIT Madras. 

2. Joint Incubation Centre (JIC): A Joint Collaboration of IIT (BHU), Varanasi and Union Bank of India established in 2023 to provide a platform where startups can learn and perform innovation in software development, quantum computing, cyber security, IoT and Data Analytics. This innovation center runs under the aegis of Ideation, Innovation and Incubation (I-3) Foundation. 

   - The event “Start-up Insights” was organized on 8th February 2025 at ABLT-4, IIT (BHU), Varanasi. The session was graced by Mr. Shirish Sathaye, General Partner, Cervin Ventures, as the Chief Guest & Keynote Speaker, along with Prof. Amit Patra, Director, IIT (BHU), as the Guest of Honour. The event brought together industry experts, investors, academicians, and entrepreneurs for insightful discussions on ideation, securing capital, and scaling ventures globally. 

   - A two-day workshop titled “Startup Insight: Lab to Market Innovation” was held on 10–11 April 2025 in collaboration with the Joint Incubation Centre (JIC) and I-3 Foundation, along with the GopalakrishnanDeshpande Centre for Innovation and Entrepreneurship (GDC), IIT Madras, under the I-NDUCT Program. The workshop focused on translating academic research into commercially viable innovations and engaged faculty, researchers, students, and aspiring entrepreneurs. The format included expert-led sessions, group discussions, and practical exercises, all designed to strengthen the entrepreneurial ecosystem within the Institute. 

   - After a thorough evaluation few faculty and student startups were selected for funding. The selected startups and approved funding were as follows: Dr. Avanish Singh Parmar – ₹5.0 Lakhs; Dr. Sanjukta Ghosh – ₹5.0 Lakhs; Dr. Ayan Haldar – ₹5.0 Lakhs; Prof. Pradeep Paik – ₹5.0 Lakhs; Mr. Kulraj Singh – ₹2.5 Lakhs; Mr. Yash – ₹2.5 Lakhs; and Mr. Akarshan Katiyaar & Ms. Riddhi – ₹0.6 Lakhs, with the total funding amounting to ₹25.6 Lakhs. Notably, both Kulraj Singh and Yash also received matching support from the M. Ramakrishnan and Malini Entrepreneurship Fund, established by an IIT (BHU) alumnus. 

3. E-Cell: Student Body of IIT (BHU) Varanasi is formed as Entrepreneurship Cell to promote entrepreneurial awareness among the students. eCell performs various capacity building programs, ideation Workshops so that the students can come up with new ideas and try to commercialize them through a start-up. 

   - The E-Summit ’25 concluded successfully with an overwhelming participation of 5000+ attendees, including startup founders, guest speakers, angel investors, venture capitalists, syndicates, and startup enablers. The 

Annual Report 2024-25 377 

Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi 

**==> picture [34 x 35] intentionally omitted <==**

summit offered a dynamic blend of panel discussions, fireside chats, startup expo, fundraising-pitching events, competitions, and networking sessions, creating a vibrant ecosystem for entrepreneurship and innovation. With 50+ startups on campus, 5000+ registrations, 45+ partners and sponsors, and 40+ speakers, investors, judges, and enablers, the event marked a significant milestone in fostering entrepreneurial growth. 

- As part of the pre-events, Startup Junction was organized on 20th December 2024 in Pune and Bengaluru, featuring 17 startups, 6 investors, and 13 partners. The event enabled curated startup–VC connects with active participation from leading funds such as Pentathlon Ventures, SpringUp Capitals, Alcor Fund, Ankur Capital, Pi Ventures, LetsVenture, Beenext, and others, providing early-stage ventures with valuable exposure and opportunities. 

- Another flagship pre-event, the Venture Capital Bootcamp (17th–26th December 2024, Online), attracted 480+ participants. Conducted by eminent venture capital professionals including Madhuri Sawant, Shashank Randev, Simran Patel, and Abhishek Kakkar, the program combined sessions, assignments, and certifications, equipping participants with deep insights into venture capital and startup investment practices. 

- The Inaugural Ceremony of E-Summit ’25, held on 31st January 2025 at ABLT-4, IIT BHU, was attended by 350+ participants. It was inaugurated by industry leaders Amit Kataria, Dhruv Agarwala, and Avi Dutt, under the theme “Vortex of Visionaries”, setting the tone for a three-day celebration of innovation and entrepreneurship. 

- The Startup Expo, held on 1st–2nd February 2025 at Rajputana Ground, featured 20+ startup stalls and witnessed a footfall of 5000+ attendees. It served as a hub for showcasing innovative products, fostering networking opportunities, and forging strategic collaborations between startups, investors, and corporates. 

- The Panel Discussions and Fireside Chats, held on 1st–2nd February 2025 at ABLT-4, featured three engaging panels with a total of 15 distinguished panelists. The discussions revolved around key themes such as Artificial Intelligence, Consumer Brands, and Venture Capital. Fireside chats brought in valuable insights from leaders including Amit Kataria (Saras AI), Dharamveer Singh (Zostel), and Dhruv Agarwala (Housing.com), who shared their entrepreneurial journeys and perspectives on emerging opportunities in the ecosystem. 

- The Million Dollar Pitch, conducted on 1st–2nd February 2025 at the Cisco ThingQbator Space, witnessed an overwhelming response with 135 registrations, out of which 14 startups were shortlisted as finalists. The event attracted participation from 9+ reputed investors and provided curated funding opportunities. Renowned investors from Trifecta Capital, Anicut Capital, GrowX, LetsVenture, Aroa Ventures, Riverwalk Holdings, Eraya, SucSEED, BrandBlaze, and others actively engaged with the startups, offering mentorship and potential funding pathways. 

- Overall, E-Summit ’25 emerged as a landmark celebration of entrepreneurship, fostering impactful engagements across startups, investors, corporates, and students. It created a thriving platform for innovation, funding opportunities, and industry–academia collaboration. The summit was successfully organized by E-Cell, IIT (BHU) under the aegis of the I-3 Foundation.

## **33. Institute Works Department (IWD)** 

Ever since its inception in the year 2014, Institute Works Department (IWD) in IIT (BHU) shoulders the onus of major/ minor repairs, maintenance, retrofitting, renovation and development of infrastructure along with proper operation and sustenance of existing utility lines. The upkeep and functioning of water distribution system, sewerage network, electrical overhead/underground cable lines, distribution sub-stations (DSSs), power sub-stations (PSSs) and SCADA system also pertain to the prime responsibility of IWD. 

In addition to maintaining hostels, the guest house, faculty quarters/apartments, and academic buildings, IWD also undertakes the development and maintenance of roadside infrastructure, including pavements and bituminous roads. 

Depending upon the extent and quantum of work, IWD floats online tenders to award work-contracts to various vendors/ contractors to execute maintenance/development related works of IIT (BHU) under compliance of GFR and standard practices of Civil/Electrical Engineering.

## **34. Central Instrument Facility (CIF)** 

**1. Overview:** The central instrument facility (CIF) is equipped with sophisticated instruments to carry out characterization and analytical needs under one roof for both external and internal users from academia and industry. Our facilities are dedicated to preserving and improving the research efficiency of global standards. This facility is well-versed in exploring structural, morphological, thermal, surface and molecular properties for a wide range of nano or microscopic materials like metals, ceramic, polymers, biomaterials and composites etc. Apart from these, centre is also extending facilities for trace analysis of metallic or non-metallic contaminants. 

CIF provides research scholars with a variety of characterization techniques that support their academic endeavours. 

||**Structural Characterization:**<br>XRD<br>XPS<br>FT-IR<br>NMR<br>Nano-Indentation||**Morphological Characterization:**<br>Confocal<br>SEM<br>TEM<br>AFM/SPM<br>Optical Microscope|
|---|---|---|---|
||**Identifcation of Molecules:**<br>FT-IR<br>NMR||**Thermal Characterization:**<br>TGA<br>DSC|
||**Electrical Characterization:**<br>VNA<br>PCB||**Surface Area Characterization**<br>BET<br>Chemisorption|
||**Magnetic Characterization**<br>Magnetic Properties Measurement System<br>(MPMS)||**Simulator**<br>Thermal Mechanical Physical Simulation and<br>Testing Machine (Gleeble)|
||**Elemental Characterization**<br>ICP-MS<br>Ion- Chromatography<br>EDS||**Mechanical / Rheological Characterization**<br>Rheometer / DMA<br>Tribometer|



Herein, each instrument is operating under expert faculty members of this institute. With these views- 

## **“Our mission is to provide futuristic research infrastructure and quality education services in support of advanced instrumentation.”** 

The CIF is providing the services to the users _via_ an easy-to-use online booking system that requires minimal human participation. 

Dr. Pralay Maiti, Professor of School of Materials Science and Technology, leads the CIF with full-time professionals and scientific staff having their specific areas of expertise. In addition, Doctoral and PG students are also giving their expertise for the smooth operation of the instrument. 

**2.** Approximate number of internal students/ users of CIF facilities: ~ **17640 samples** 

**3.** Number of Institute Department/School user of CIF facilities: **15 Departments/Schools**

|||
|12|DSC|

|16|ICP-MS|

|22|Rheometer/DMA|
|23|Nano-Indentation|



These include morphological imaging of materials’ surface resolution up to 5nm magnification range with elemental analysis. It covers a range of macro-to-micro materials of biological or non-biological types with the help of SEM, HR-SEM, HR-TEM and SPM. Room temperature or high temperature-based structural and thermal characterization is possible by XRD and TGA/DSC analyzer, respectively. The functional and bonding environments of nearly all elements are possible to analyze by the combination of measurements based on FTIR, NMR and XPS system. Magnetic properties of all type of magnetic materials are possible by MPMS. Wear properties of all materials are able to perform on multi-function tribometer based on both ball-on-disk and pin-on-disk method. Surface active area with pore volume and its distribution of porous materials is also possible to analyze by BET/Chemisorption measurement system. Complete ranges of trace analysis of WHO recommended contaminants are able to perform on ICP-MS and Ion chromatography. In addition, recently Confocal laser Scanning Microscope is introduced in order to observe the location of fluorescent moieties present in biological or any matrix system. Additionally, a Vector Network Analyzer is used for measuring network parameters in RF engineering. Thus, instrumental facilities present herein are able to elaborate on any properties of concerned materials. A recent addition to our simulation capabilities is the Thermal-Mechanical Physical Simulator and Testing Equipment (TMPST), known as the Gleeble system. It performs regulated laboratory-based simulations of multi-stage metal forming processes at high temperatures and high deformation rates, similar to those in rolling mills and large-scale metal production. At the end of the financial year FY 2024-25, two new sophisticated instrument have been added/installed to the CIF namely Rheometer/DMA and Nano-Indentation. These new additions will facilitate the CIF to test the Rheology as well as Hardness of samples.

## **35. Supercomputing Centre (Param Shivay Supercomputer)** 

**Year of Establishment:** 19[th] February, 2019, **Functional from:** 1[st] December 2019 

**Coordinator during 2024-25:** Dr. Hari Prabhat Gupta 

**Coordinator at present:** Prof. Ravi Shankar Singh 

**1. Introduction:** Param Shivay is a high-performance supercomputer installed at the IIT(BHU), Varanasi. It is part of the National Supercomputing Mission (NSM), which aims to enhance the research capabilities of India’s educational and research institutions by providing them with advanced computational resources. This is the first HPC system deployed at IIT(BHU), Varanasi under the NSM build approach which was inaugurated by the Honorable Prime Minister of India, Shri Narendra Modi. As the first system under NSM, it boasts a very good number of external users, including an approved project under NSM-APP. Currently, the total number of successful jobs submitted using this system is 11,32,891. The total number of users on the PARAM Shivay facility is 1500, including 1250 host institute 

users, 100 external users, and 102 NSM-APP users. In the year 2024, a total of 2000 technical support tickets were successfully resolved through the online ticketing and email support system. 

**2. Architecture:** The server is equipped with a high-end platform based on cutting-edge technologies in terms of processing power and storage. The computation parts (CPU, GPU nodes) are based on highperformance processors from Intel and NVIDIA. The storage is built on the DDN collaborating with ATOS for the high-end Bull Sequana platform specifically customized for HPC on AI and research computations. The PCI interconnects are Mellanox InfiniBand interconnects. The PARAM Shivay supercomputer is designed with a heterogeneous and hybrid configuration that combines Intel Xeon Skylake processors and NVIDIA  Tesla  V100.  The  HPC Technologies team at the Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (C-DAC) designed and implemented the system, which includes 2 Master nodes, 4 Login nodes, 4 Service nodes, and 223 (CPU+GPU) nodes with a total peak computing capacity of 837 (CPU+GPU) TFLOPS performance. The cluster is made up of compute nodes connected with Mellanox (ERD) InfiniBand interconnect network, and it uses the Lustre parallel file system. 

## **3. Applications and Domains:** We cater 

to a wide range of applications and target 

Annual Report 2024-25 389 

Indian Institute of Technology (BHU) Varanasi 

**==> picture [34 x 35] intentionally omitted <==**

domains, as our facilities are utilized by researchers and faculty from various disciplines across different institutions for their computationally intensive tasks. The research conducted on the server spans several domains, notably including Molecular Dynamics for Chemical Science, Biomedical Science, Pharmaceutics, and Material Science; Computational Fluid Dynamics for Computational Physics, Mechanical, and Chemical domains; and Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, and Deep Learning. The chart presented here shows the domain involvement on the server. The system has proven invaluable for research needs across diverse scientific areas, including Computational Fluid Dynamics, Computational Chemistry, Artificial Intelligence, Computational Physics, Manufacturing Process Modeling, Computational Biology, Data Science, Weather & Climate, Oil & Gas, Seismic, Life, and Material Sciences. Below is a list of the most popular and highly demanded applications on the server. 

**==> picture [450 x 271] intentionally omitted <==**

## **4. Major Outcomes** 

- **4.1 Publications and acknowledgments:** Over 200 publications across various fields have acknowledged the computational support provided by the Param Shivay facility. These publications span different domains and from different Institutions. All external institutions benefitted from our facility have acknowledged Param Shivay in their publications. 

- **4.2 Institutions benefitted from Param Shivay:** Along with IIT(BHU), Varanasi and BHU Varanasi, around 37 other research institutes are using the Param Shivay server.

**==> picture [34 x 35] intentionally omitted <==**

- **4.3 Cluster Utilization statistics:** The current server usage is sound, with a utilization ratio exceeding 75% for CPU/GPU nodes. This high usage results in some larger jobs experiencing wait times, with the average waiting time for GPU access being 1 to 2 days. To manage this load, we have requested an expansion of the facility. 

- **4.4 Events and Visits:** Over a period of six months, we successfully conducted the Supercomputing Workshop Series, which facilitated knowledge transfer on both foundational and advanced concepts in High Performance Computing (HPC), including MPI/OpenMP and CUDA programming. In addition, we hosted a number of educational visits from various schools, colleges, and institutions, aimed at introducing young students to the significance and potential of supercomputing technologies. Notable among these were delegations from Vidyasagar University (Midnapore), Sunbeam College for Women (Varanasi), PM Shri Composite School (Gangapur), Inspiria Knowledge Campus (Siliguri), and PM Shri JNV Sinhachawar (Ballia). 

- **4.5 In-process of Proposal for NSM 2.0 server with more than double capacity:** A new system has been proposed with more than double the present capacity. The institute that has successfully managed to run the facility for the last 6 years. 

## **5. People at the Supercomputing Centre** 

|**Sr.**<br>**No.**|**Name**|**Designation**|
|---|---|---|
|1.|Prof. Ravi Shankar Singh|Coordinator|
|2.|Mrs. Nisha Singh|System Analyst|



**6. Conclusion:** The usage statistics and key deliverables such as publications and collaborations with external institutions clearly indicate that users are effectively utilizing the Param Shivay facility. The system has significantly supported researchers across diverse domains and institutions, solidifying its role as an essential resource in advanced computational research. To further improve performance and reduce response times, we plan to upgrade the system with enhanced computational power and expanded storage. Our objective is to strengthen the facility’s capabilities and visibility while increasing awareness of its strategic importance.

## **36. Main Workshop** 

## **Year of Establishment: 1919** 

**Head of the Department:** Prof. Mohd Zaheer Khan Yusufzai, Professor In-Charge, Main Workshop, IIT (BHU). w.e.f. 01.07.2025 

## **Brief Introduction of the Department including major research area and major infrastructure:** 

Main Workshop, IIT (BHU) aims to impart scientific, technical, and professional knowledge combined with necessary practical training to the students, which is necessary for their professional development. The knowledge imparted can make the students fit for current and indigenous industries and help develop in them entrepreneurial skills and thus providing useful resources for the country. IIT (BHU) Main Workshop used to produce engineering items using different machine tools. The unit also provides technical assistance for the maintenance and fabrication of essential items, to be used in-house and for users outside I.I.T.(BHU). This unit works in synergy with the teaching departments like the Mechanical Engineering, Metallurgical Engineering, etc., by providing them technical support in terms of machines and manpower, and getting benefited by their knowledge. The Main Workshop also provides technical and on-the-job training to the less privileged section of society. The workshop also houses and maintains advanced manufacturing facilities like Wire Electric Discharge Machine (WEDM), which facilitates not only the students and faculty members but also the nearby Industries (on a chargeable basis).

## **Head of the Department: Prof. Santosh Kumar**
